verses.speaker: धृतराष्ट्र
verses.text: त्या पवित्र कुरु-क्षेत्रीं पांडूचे आणि आमुचे । युध्दार्थ जमले तेव्हां वर्तलें काय संजया ?
verses.roman: tyā pavitr kuru-kṣētrīm pāṇḍūcē āṇi āmucē । yudhdārth jamalē tēvhāṁ vartalēṅ kāy sañjayā ?
verses.summary: Dhritarashtra asks Sanjaya what his sons and the Pandavas did once they gathered to fight on the field of Kurukshetra.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: पाहिली पांडवी सेना सज्ज दुर्योधनें तिथें । मग गेला गुरुपाशीं त्यास हें वाक्य बोलिला
verses.roman: pāhilī pāṇḍavī sēnā sajj duryōdhanēn tithēṁ । mag gēlā gurupāśīn tyās hēṁ vāky bōlilā
verses.summary: Sanjaya says Duryodhana, seeing the Pandava army arrayed, went to his teacher Drona and spoke to him.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: गुरुजी तुमचा शिष्य शहाणा द्रुपदात्मज । विशाळ रचिलें त्यानें पहा पांडव सैन्य हें
verses.roman: gurujī tumacā śiṣy śahāṇā drupadātmaj । viśāḷ racilēn tyānēm pahā pāṇḍav sainy hēṁ
verses.summary: Duryodhana tells Drona to behold the vast Pandava army, arranged by Drupada's son, his clever student.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: ह्यांत शूर धनुर्धारी युध्दीं भीमार्जुनांसह । महा-रथी तो द्रुपद विराट नृप सात्यकी
verses.roman: hyānt śūr dhanurdhārī yudhdīm bhīmārjunāṁsah । mahā-rathī tō drupad virāṭ nr̥p sātyakī
verses.summary: Duryodhana points out heroic archers equal to Bhima and Arjuna—Drupada, Virata, and Satyaki, all great warriors.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: धृष्टकेतु तसा शूर काश्य तो चेकितान हि । पुरुजित कुंतिभोजीय आणि शैब्य नरोत्तम
verses.roman: dhr̥ṣṭakētu tasā śūr kāśy tō cēkitān hi । purujit kuntibhōjīy āṇi śaiby narōttam
verses.summary: He names more warriors: Dhrishtaketu, Cekitana, the king of Kashi, Purujit, Kuntibhoja, and the noble Shaibya.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: उत्तमौजा हि तो वीर युधामन्यु हि विक्रमी । सौभद्र आणि ते पुत्र द्रौपदीचे महा-रथी
verses.roman: uttamaujā hi tō vīr yudhāmanyu hi vikramī । saubhadr āṇi tē putr draupadīcē mahā-rathī
verses.summary: He lists Yudhamanyu, Uttamauja, Abhimanyu, and Draupadi's sons—all mighty warriors.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: आतां जे आमुच्यांतील सैन्याचे मुख्य नायक । सांगतों जाणण्यासाठी घ्यावें लक्षांत आपण
verses.roman: ātāñ jē āmucyāntīl sainyācē mukhy nāyak । sāṅgatōñ jāṇaṇyāsāṭhī ghyāvēṁ lakṣānt āpaṇ
verses.summary: Duryodhana turns to name the chief leaders of his own army for Drona's attention.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: स्वतां आपण ह भीष्म यशस्वी कृप कर्ण तो । अश्वत्थामा सौमदत्ती जयद्रथ विकर्ण हि
verses.roman: svatāṁ āpaṇ h bhīṣm yaśasvī kr̥p karṇ tō । aśvatthāmā saumadattī jayadrath vikarṇ hi
verses.summary: He names Drona himself, Bhishma, Karna, Kripa, Ashvatthama, Bhurishrava, Jayadratha, and Vikarna.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: अनेक दुसरे वीर माझ्यासाठी मरावया । सजले सर्व शस्त्रानीं झुंजणारे प्रवीण जे
verses.roman: anēk dusarē vīr mājhyāsāṭhī marāvayā । sajalē sarv śastrānīñ jhuñjaṇārē pravīṇ jē
verses.summary: He says many other heroes, skilled and armed, are ready to lay down their lives for him.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: अफाट आमुचें सैन्य भीष्मानीं रक्षिलें असे । मोजकें पांडवांचें हें भीमानें रक्षिलें असे
verses.roman: aphāṭ āmucēṁ sainy bhīṣmānīṁ rakṣilēṁ asē । mōjakēm pāṇḍavāñcēṁ hēm bhīmānēṁ rakṣilēṁ asē
verses.summary: Duryodhana notes his vast army is guarded by Bhishma while the smaller Pandava force is guarded by Bhima.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: राहूनि आपुल्या स्थानीं जेथ ज्यास नियोजिलें । चहूंकडूनि भीष्मांस रक्षाल सगळेजण
verses.roman: rāhūni āpulyā sthānīñ jēth jyās niyōjilēṁ । cahūṅkaḍūni bhīṣmāṁs rakṣāl sagaḷējaṇ
verses.summary: He urges all to hold their assigned positions and protect Bhishma on every side.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: हर्षवीत चि तों त्यास सिंह नाद करुनियां । प्रतापी वृध्द भीष्मानीं मोठयानें शंख फुंकिला
verses.roman: harṣavīt ci tōn tyās siṁh nād karuniyāṁ । pratāpī vr̥dhd bhīṣmānīm mōṭhayānēṁ śaṅkh phuṅkilā
verses.summary: To cheer Duryodhana, the aged Bhishma roared like a lion and loudly blew his conch.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: तत्क्षणी शंखभेर्यादि रण-वाद्यें विचित्र चि । एकत्र झडलीं तेव्हां झाला शब्द भयंकर ।
verses.roman: tatkṣaṇī śaṅkhabhēryādi raṇa-vādyēṁ vicitr ci । ēkatr jhaḍalīn tēvhāñ jhālā śabd bhayaṅkar ।
verses.summary: At once conches, drums, and battle instruments sounded together, making a terrifying din.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: इकडे दिव्य घोडयांच्या मोठया भव्य रथांतुनी । माधवें अर्जुनें दिव्य फुंकिले शंख आपुले
verses.roman: ikaḍē divy ghōḍayāñcyā mōṭhayā bhavy rathāntunī । mādhavēṁ arjunēn divy phuṅkilē śaṅkh āpulē
verses.summary: On the other side, Krishna and Arjuna, in their great chariot drawn by white horses, blew their conches.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: पांच-जन्य हृषीकेशें देव-दत्त धनंजयें । पौंड्र तो फुंकिला भीमें महा-शंख महा-बळें
verses.roman: pāñca-jany hr̥ṣīkēśēn dēva-datt dhanañjayēṁ । pauṇḍr tō phuṅkilā bhīmēm mahā-śaṅkh mahā-baḷēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna blew Panchajanya, Arjuna blew Devadatta, and mighty Bhima blew his great conch Paundra.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: तेव्हां अनंत-विजय धर्मराज युधिष्ठिरें । नकुळें सहदेवेंहि सुघोष मणि-पुष्पक ।
verses.roman: tēvhāṁ ananta-vijay dharmarāj yudhiṣṭhirēṁ । nakuḷēṁ sahadēvēṁhi sughōṣ maṇi-puṣpak ।
verses.summary: Yudhishthira blew Anantavijaya; Nakula and Sahadeva blew Sughosha and Manipushpaka.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: मग काश्य धनुर्धारी शिखंडी हि महा-रथी । विराट आणि सेनानी तसा अजित सात्यकि
verses.roman: mag kāśy dhanurdhārī śikhaṇḍī hi mahā-rathī । virāṭ āṇi sēnānī tasā ajit sātyaki
verses.summary: The king of Kashi, the archer Shikhandi, Virata, the unconquered Satyaki, and others joined in.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: राजा द्रुपद सौभद्र द्रौपदीचे हि पुत्र ते । सर्वांनी फुंकिले शंख आपुले वेगवेगळे ।
verses.roman: rājā drupad saubhadr draupadīcē hi putr tē । sarvānnī phuṅkilē śaṅkh āpulē vēgavēgaḷē ।
verses.summary: King Drupada, Abhimanyu, and Draupadi's sons all blew their separate conches.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: त्या घोषें कौरवांची तें हृदयेंचि विदारिली । भरुनि भूमि आकाश गाजला तो भयंकर
verses.roman: tyā ghōṣēṅ kauravāñcī tēṁ hr̥dayēñci vidārilī । bharuni bhūmi ākāś gājalā tō bhayaṅkar
verses.summary: The tumultuous sound filling earth and sky tore the hearts of Dhritarashtra's sons.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: मग नीट उभे सारे पुन्हां कौरव राहिले । चाळणार पुढें शस्त्रें इतुक्यांत कपि ध्वज
verses.roman: mag nīṭ ubhē sārē punhāṅ kaurav rāhilē । cāḷaṇār puḍhēṁ śastrēṁ itukyānt kapi dhvaj
verses.summary: As the Kauravas stood ready to launch weapons, Arjuna, whose banner bore Hanuman, raised his bow.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: हाती धनुष्य घेउनी बोले कृष्णास वाक्य हें ॥ दोन्ही सैन्यांमधें कृष्णा माझा रथ उभा करी
verses.roman: hātī dhanuṣy ghēunī bōlē kr̥ṣṇās vāky hēṁ ॥ dōnhī sainyāmmadhēṅ kr̥ṣṇā mājhā rath ubhā karī
verses.summary: Arjuna takes up his bow and tells Krishna to place his chariot between the two armies.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: म्हणजे कोण पाहीन राखिती युध्द-कामना । आज ह्या रण-संग्रामीं कोणाशी झुंजणे मज
verses.roman: mhaṇajē kōṇ pāhīn rākhitī yudhda-kāmanā । āj hyā raṇa-saṅgrāmīṅ kōṇāśī jhuñjaṇē maj
verses.summary: Arjuna says he wants to see who stands ready to fight and whom he must face in this battle.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: झुंजते वीर ते सारे घेतों पाहूनि येथ मीं । युध्दीं त्या हत-बुध्दीचें जे करूं पाहती प्रिय ।
verses.roman: jhuñjatē vīr tē sārē ghētōm pāhūni yēth mīṁ । yudhdīn tyā hata-budhdīcēñ jē karūm pāhatī priy ।
verses.summary: He wishes to see those gathered to fight, eager to please the wrong-minded Duryodhana.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: अर्जुनाचें असें वाक्य कृष्णें ऐकूनि शीघ्र चि । दोन्ही सैन्यामधें केला उभा उत्तम तो रथ
verses.roman: arjunācēṁ asēṁ vāky kr̥ṣṇēṁ aikūni śīghr ci । dōnhī sainyāmadhēṅ kēlā ubhā uttam tō rath
verses.summary: Sanjaya says Krishna, hearing Arjuna, swiftly placed the fine chariot between the two armies.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: मग लक्षूनियां नीट भीष्म-द्रोण-नृपांस तो । म्हणे हे जमले पार्था पहा कौरव सर्व तूं ।
verses.roman: mag lakṣūniyān nīṭ bhīṣma-drōṇa-nr̥pāṁs tō । mhaṇē hē jamalē pārthā pahā kaurav sarv tūṁ ।
verses.summary: Facing Bhishma, Drona, and the kings, Krishna tells Arjuna to behold all the gathered Kauravas.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: तेथ अर्जुन तो पाहे उभे सारे व्यवस्थित । आजे काके तसे मामे सासरे सोयरे सखे
verses.roman: tēth arjun tō pāhē ubhē sārē vyavasthit । ājē kākē tasē māmē sāsarē sōyarē sakhē
verses.summary: Arjuna sees standing there his grandfathers, uncles, fathers-in-law, kinsmen, and friends.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: गुरु बंधु मुलें नातू दोन्ही सैन्यांत सारखे । असे पाहूनि तो सारे सज्ज बांधव आपुले॥
verses.roman: guru bandhu mulēn nātū dōnhī sainyānt sārakhē । asē pāhūni tō sārē sajj bāndhav āpulē॥
verses.summary: He sees teachers, brothers, sons, and grandsons—his own kin arrayed in both armies.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: अत्यंत करुणा-ग्रस्त विषादें वाक्य बोलिला ॥ कृष्णा स्वजन हे सारे युध्दीं उत्सुक पाहुनी
verses.roman: atyant karuṇā-grast viṣādēṁ vāky bōlilā ॥ kr̥ṣṇā svajan hē sārē yudhdīṁ utsuk pāhunī
verses.summary: Seeing his kinsmen eager to fight, Arjuna, overcome with pity and grief, begins to speak.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: गात्रेंचि गळती माझीं होतसे तोंड कोरडे । शरीरीं सुटतो कंप उभे रोमांच राहती
verses.roman: gātrēñci gaḷatī mājhīṁ hōtasē tōṇḍ kōraḍē । śarīrīṁ suṭatō kamp ubhē rōmāñc rāhatī
verses.summary: Arjuna says his limbs fail, his mouth dries, his body trembles, and his hair stands on end.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: गांडीव न टिके हातीं सगळी गळते त्वचा । न शकेंचि उभा राहूं मन हें भ्रमलें जसें
verses.roman: gāṇḍīv n ṭikē hātīṁ sagaḷī gaḷatē tvacā । n śakēñci ubhā rāhūm man hēm bhramalēñ jasēṁ
verses.summary: His bow Gandiva slips from his hand, his skin burns, he cannot stand, and his mind reels.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: कृष्णा मी पाहतों सारीं विपरीत चि लक्षणें । कल्याण न दिसे युध्दीं स्व-जनांस वधूनियां ।
verses.roman: kr̥ṣṇā mī pāhatōṁ sārīṁ viparīt ci lakṣaṇēṁ । kalyāṇ n disē yudhdīṁ sva-janāṁs vadhūniyāṁ ।
verses.summary: Arjuna sees ill omens and foresees no good in killing his own kinsmen in battle.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: नको जय नको राज्य नकोत मज तीं सुखें । राज्यें भोगें मिळे काय किंवा काय जगूनि हि
verses.roman: nakō jay nakō rājy nakōt maj tīṁ sukhēṁ । rājyēm bhōgēm miḷē kāy kiṁvā kāy jagūni hi
verses.summary: He says he wants no victory, kingdom, or pleasures—what use are they, or even life itself?

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: ज्यांच्यासाठीं अपेक्षावीं राज्य भोग सुखें हि तीं । सजले ते चि युध्दास धना-प्राणांस सोडुनी
verses.roman: jyāñcyāsāṭhīṁ apēkṣāvīṁ rājy bhōg sukhēṁ hi tīṁ । sajalē tē ci yudhdās dhanā-prāṇāṁs sōḍunī
verses.summary: Those for whose sake one would want kingdom and pleasures are themselves here, risking life and wealth.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: आजे बाप मुलें नातू आमुचे दिसती इथें । सासरे मेहुणे मामे संबंधी आणि हे गुरु
verses.roman: ājē bāp mulēn nātū āmucē disatī ithēṁ । sāsarē mēhuṇē māmē sambandhī āṇi hē guru
verses.summary: He names teachers, fathers, sons, grandsons, uncles, and in-laws standing before him.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: न मारुं इच्छितों ह्यांस मारितील जरी मज । विश्व साम्राज्य सोडीन पृथ्वीचा पाड तो किती
verses.roman: n māruṁ icchitōṁ hyāṁs māritīl jarī maj । viśv sāmrājy sōḍīn pr̥thvīcā pāḍ tō kitī
verses.summary: Arjuna will not kill them even to win the three worlds, let alone earthly rule.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: हया कौरवांस मारुनि कायसें आमुचें प्रिय अत्याचारी जरी झाले ह्यांस मारुनि पाप चि
verses.roman: hayā kauravāṁs māruni kāyasēṁ āmucēm priy atyācārī jarī jhālē hyāṁs māruni pāp ci
verses.summary: He asks what joy killing the Kauravas could bring; killing these aggressors would only bring sin.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: म्हणुनि घात बंधूंचा आम्हां योग्य नव्हे चि तो । आम्ही स्वजन मारुनि सुखी व्हावें कसें बरें
verses.roman: mhaṇuni ghāt bandhūñcā āmhāṁ yōgy navhē ci tō । āmhī svajan māruni sukhī vhāvēṅ kasēm barēṁ
verses.summary: He says slaying his kinsmen is wrong and could never make them happy.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: लोभानें नासली बुध्दी त्यामुळें हें न पाहती । मित्र-द्रोही कसें पाप काय दोष कुल-क्षयीं
verses.roman: lōbhānēn nāsalī budhdī tyāmuḷēṁ hēn n pāhatī । mitra-drōhī kasēm pāp kāy dōṣ kula-kṣayīṁ
verses.summary: He says the Kauravas, blinded by greed, don't see the sin in destroying family and betraying friends.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: परी हें पाप टाळावें आम्हां कां समजूं नये । कुल क्षयीं महा-दोष कृष्णा उघड पाहतां ।
verses.roman: parī hēm pāp ṭāḷāvēṁ āmhāṅ kāṁ samajūn nayē । kul kṣayīm mahā-dōṣ kr̥ṣṇā ughaḍ pāhatāṁ ।
verses.summary: Arjuna asks why they shouldn't know to avoid this sin, since the evil of destroying a family is plain.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: कुल क्षयें लया जाती कुल-धर्म सनातन । धर्मनाशें कुळीं सर्व अधर्म पसरे मग
verses.roman: kul kṣayēṁ layā jātī kula-dharm sanātan । dharmanāśēṅ kuḷīṁ sarv adharm pasarē mag
verses.summary: He says when a family is destroyed its ancient traditions perish, and lawlessness overtakes the rest.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: अधर्म माजतो तेंव्हा भ्रष्ट होती कुल-स्त्रिया । स्त्रिया बिघडतां कृष्णा घडतो वर्ण-संकर
verses.roman: adharm mājatō tēṁvhā bhraṣṭ hōtī kula-striyā । striyā bighaḍatāṅ kr̥ṣṇā ghaḍatō varṇa-saṅkar
verses.summary: When lawlessness spreads, the women of the family become corrupted, leading to confusion of social order.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: संकरें नरका जाय कुलघ्नांसह तें कुल । पितरांचा अधःपात होतसे श्राध्द लोपुनी ।
verses.roman: saṅkarēn narakā jāy kulaghnāṁsah tēṅ kul । pitarāñcā adhaḥpāt hōtasē śrādhd lōpunī ।
verses.summary: Such confusion drags the family and its destroyers to hell, and ancestors fall as offerings cease.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: ह्या दोषानीं कुलघ्नांच्या होउनी वर्ण संकर । जातीचे बुडती धर्म कुळाचे हि सनातन
verses.roman: hyā dōṣānīṅ kulaghnāñcyā hōunī varṇ saṅkar । jātīcē buḍatī dharm kuḷācē hi sanātan
verses.summary: These crimes of family-destroyers cause caste-mixing and ruin the eternal duties of caste and family.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: ज्यानीं बुडविले धर्म कुळाचे त्यांस निश्चित । नरकीं राहणें लागे आलों ऐकत हें असे
verses.roman: jyānīm buḍavilē dharm kuḷācē tyāṁs niścit । narakīṁ rāhaṇēṁ lāgē ālōṁ aikat hēṁ asē
verses.summary: Arjuna says he has heard that those who destroy family duties dwell forever in hell.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: अरेरे केवढें पाप आम्ही आरंभिलें असे । लोभें राज्य-सुखासाठीं मारावें स्व-जनांस जें
verses.roman: arērē kēvaḍhēm pāp āmhī ārambhilēṁ asē । lōbhēṁ rājya-sukhāsāṭhīm mārāvēṁ sva-janāṁs jēṁ
verses.summary: He laments the great sin they are about to commit—ready to kill kin out of greed for kingdom and pleasure.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: त्याहूनि शस्त्र सोडूनि उगा राहीन तें बरें । मारोत मग हे युध्दीं शस्त्रानीं मज कौरव
verses.roman: tyāhūni śastr sōḍūni ugā rāhīn tēm barēṁ । mārōt mag hē yudhdīṁ śastrānīm maj kaurav
verses.summary: He says it would be better to let the Kauravas kill him, unarmed and unresisting, than to fight.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: असें रणांत बोलूनि शोकावेगांत अर्जुन । धनुष्य-बाण टाकूनि रथीं बैसूनि राहिला
verses.roman: asēṁ raṇānt bōlūni śōkāvēgānt arjun । dhanuṣya-bāṇ ṭākūni rathīm baisūni rāhilā
verses.summary: Sanjaya says Arjuna, having spoken thus in grief, cast away his bow and arrows and sank down in his chariot.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: असा तो करुणा-ग्रस्त घाबरा अश्रु गाळित । करीत असतां खेद त्यास हें कृष्ण बोलिला
verses.roman: asā tō karuṇā-grast ghābarā aśru gāḷit । karīt asatāṅ khēd tyās hēṅ kr̥ṣṇ bōlilā
verses.summary: Sanjaya reports that Krishna spoke to the grief-stricken, tearful, despairing Arjuna.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: कोठूनि भलत्या वेळीं सुचलें पाप हें तुज । असें न सुचे थोरांस ह्यानें दुष्कीर्ति दुर्गति
verses.roman: kōṭhūni bhalatyā vēḷīṁ sucalēm pāp hēn tuj । asēn n sucē thōrāṁs hyānēn duṣkīrti durgati
verses.summary: Krishna asks Arjuna where this weakness came from at such a moment; it brings disgrace and ruin, unworthy of a noble man.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: निर्विर्य तूं नको होऊं न शोभे हें मुळीं तुज । भिकार दुबळी वृत्ति सोडुनी ऊठ तूं कसा ॥
verses.roman: nirviry tūn nakō hōūn n śōbhē hēm muḷīn tuj । bhikār dubaḷī vr̥tti sōḍunī ūṭh tūṅ kasā ॥
verses.summary: Krishna tells Arjuna not to become a weakling; this petty faintheartedness doesn't suit him, so he should shake it off and rise.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: कसा रणांगणीं झुंजूं भीष्म-द्रोणांविरुध्द मी । ह्यांस बाण कसे मारुं आम्हां हे पूजनीय कीं
verses.roman: kasā raṇāṅgaṇīñ jhuñjūm bhīṣma-drōṇāṁvirudhd mī । hyāṁs bāṇ kasē māruṁ āmhāṁ hē pūjanīy kīṁ
verses.summary: Arjuna asks how he can fight and shoot arrows at Bhishma and Drona, who are worthy of his worship.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: न मारितां थोर गुरुंस येथें । भिक्षा हि मागूनि भलें जगावें ॥ हितेच्छु हे ह्यांस वधूनि भोग । भोगूं कसे भगुर रक्त-मिश्र
verses.roman: n māritān thōr guruṁs yēthēṁ । bhikṣā hi māgūni bhalēñ jagāvēṁ ॥ hitēcchu hē hyāṁs vadhūni bhōg । bhōgūṅ kasē bhagur rakta-miśr
verses.summary: Arjuna says it would be better to live by begging than to kill these honored elders; killing them would taint any enjoyment with blood.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: ह्यांचा चि व्हावा जय आमुचा कीं कशांत कल्याण असें न जाणों । मारुनि ज्यातें जगणें न इच्छूं । झुंजावया ते चि उभे समोर ।
verses.roman: hyāñcā ci vhāvā jay āmucā kīṅ kaśānt kalyāṇ asēn n jāṇōṁ । māruni jyātēñ jagaṇēn n icchūṁ । jhuñjāvayā tē ci ubhē samōr ।
verses.summary: Arjuna says he doesn't know which outcome is better—victory or defeat—and that the very people he wouldn't want to outlive stand before him to fight.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: दैन्यानें ती मारिली वृत्ति माझी । धर्माचें ते नाशिलें ज्ञान मोहें । कैसें माझें श्रेय होईल सांगा । पायापाशीं पातलों शिष्य-भावें
verses.roman: dainyānēn tī mārilī vr̥tti mājhī । dharmācēn tē nāśilēñ jñān mōhēṁ । kaisēm mājhēṁ śrēy hōīl sāṅgā । pāyāpāśīm pātalōṁ śiṣya-bhāvēṁ
verses.summary: Overcome by pity and confusion, Arjuna asks Krishna to tell him what is truly good, surrendering as a disciple.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: मिळेल निष्कंटक राज्य येथें । लाभेल इंद्रासन देव-लोकीं । शमेल त्यानें न तथापि शोक । जो इंद्रियांतें सुकवीत माझ्या ।
verses.roman: miḷēl niṣkaṇṭak rājy yēthēṁ । lābhēl indrāsan dēva-lōkīṁ । śamēl tyānēn n tathāpi śōk । jō indriyāntēṁ sukavīt mājhyā ।
verses.summary: Arjuna says even an unrivaled kingdom or lordship over the gods wouldn't remove the grief that withers his senses.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: असें अर्जुन तो वीर हृषीकेशास बोलुनी । शेवटीं मी न झुंजेचि ह्या शब्दें स्तब्ध राहिला
verses.roman: asēṁ arjun tō vīr hr̥ṣīkēśās bōlunī । śēvaṭīm mī n jhuñjēci hyā śabdēṁ stabdh rāhilā
verses.summary: Sanjaya reports that after saying "I will not fight," Arjuna fell silent.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: मग त्यास हृषीकेश जणूं हसत बोलिला । करीत असतां शोक दोन्ही सैन्यांत तो तसा ॥
verses.roman: mag tyās hr̥ṣīkēś jaṇūṁ hasat bōlilā । karīt asatāṁ śōk dōnhī sainyānt tō tasā ॥
verses.summary: Sanjaya says Krishna, as if smiling, began to speak to the despondent Arjuna standing between the two armies.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: करिसी भलता शोक वरी ज्ञान हि सांगसी मेल्याजित्यांविषीं शोक ज्ञानवंत न जाणती
verses.roman: karisī bhalatā śōk varī jñān hi sāṅgasī mēlyājityāṁviṣīṁ śōk jñānavant n jāṇatī
verses.summary: Krishna says Arjuna grieves needlessly while speaking words of wisdom; the wise mourn neither the living nor the dead.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: मी तूं आणिक हे राजे न मागें नव्हतों कधीं । तसेचि सगळे आम्ही न पुढेंहि नसूं कधीं
verses.roman: mī tūṁ āṇik hē rājē n māgēn navhatōṅ kadhīṁ । tasēci sagaḷē āmhī n puḍhēṁhi nasūṅ kadhīṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says he, Arjuna, and these kings have always existed and will always exist.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ह्या देहीं बाल्य तारुण्य जरा वा लाभते जशी । तसा लाभे नवा देह न डगे धीर तो तिथें ।
verses.roman: hyā dēhīm bāly tāruṇy jarā vā lābhatē jaśī । tasā lābhē navā dēh n ḍagē dhīr tō tithēṁ ।
verses.summary: Krishna says just as the soul passes through childhood, youth, and age in this body, it takes on a new body; the wise are not disturbed.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: शीतोष्ण विषय-स्पर्ष सुख-दुःखांत घालिती । करी सहन तूं सारे येती जाती अनित्य ते
verses.roman: śītōṣṇ viṣaya-sparṣ sukha-duḥkhānt ghālitī । karī sahan tūṁ sārē yētī jātī anity tē
verses.summary: Krishna says sense-contacts bring heat, cold, pleasure, and pain; they come and go and are impermanent, so endure them.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ह्यांची मात्रा न चालेचि ज्या धीर पुरुषावरी । सम देखे सुखेंदुःखें मोक्ष-लाभास योग्य तो ॥
verses.roman: hyāñcī mātrā n cālēci jyā dhīr puruṣāvarī । sam dēkhē sukhēnduḥkhēm mōkṣa-lābhās yōgy tō ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says the steady person whom these don't affect, who sees pleasure and pain alike, is fit for liberation.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: नसे मिथ्यास अस्तित्व नसे सत्यास नाश हि । निवाडा देखिला संतीं ह्या दोहींचा अशापरी
verses.roman: nasē mithyās astitv nasē satyās nāś hi । nivāḍā dēkhilā santīṁ hyā dōhīñcā aśāparī
verses.summary: Krishna says the unreal has no existence and the real never ceases; sages have seen this conclusion about both.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ज्यानें हें व्यापिलें सर्व तें जाण अविनाश तूं । नाश त्या नित्य तत्वाचा कोणी हि न करूं शके
verses.roman: jyānēṁ hēṁ vyāpilēṁ sarv tēñ jāṇ avināś tūṁ । nāś tyā nity tatvācā kōṇī hi n karūṁ śakē
verses.summary: Krishna says that which pervades all is indestructible; no one can destroy this eternal reality.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: विनाशी देह हे सारे बोलिले त्यांत शाश्वत । नित्य निःसीम तो आत्मा अर्जुना झुंज ह्यास्तव ।
verses.roman: vināśī dēh hē sārē bōlilē tyānt śāśvat । nity niḥsīm tō ātmā arjunā jhuñj hyāstav ।
verses.summary: Krishna says these bodies are perishable while the embodied soul is eternal and boundless; therefore Arjuna should fight.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: जो म्हणे मारितो आत्मा आणि जो मरतो म्हणे । दोघे न जाणिती कांही न मारी न मरे चि हा
verses.roman: jō mhaṇē māritō ātmā āṇi jō maratō mhaṇē । dōghē n jāṇitī kāṁhī n mārī n marē ci hā
verses.summary: Krishna says one who thinks the soul kills or is killed understands nothing; it neither kills nor is killed.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: न जन्म पावे न कदापि मृत्यू । होऊनि मागें न पुढें न होय ॥ आला न गेला स्थिर हा पुराण । मारोत देहास परी मरे ना
verses.roman: n janm pāvē n kadāpi mr̥tyū । hōūni māgēn n puḍhēn n hōy ॥ ālā n gēlā sthir hā purāṇ । mārōt dēhās parī marē nā
verses.summary: Krishna says the soul is never born and never dies; ancient and unchanging, it is not slain when the body is slain.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: निर्विकार चि हा नित्य जन्म-मृत्यूहुनी पर । जाणें हें तत्व तो कैसा मारी वा मारवी कुणा ।
verses.roman: nirvikār ci hā nity janma-mr̥tyūhunī par । jāṇēṁ hēn tatv tō kaisā mārī vā māravī kuṇā ।
verses.summary: Krishna asks how one who knows the soul as unchanging, eternal, and beyond birth and death could kill or cause anyone to be killed.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: सांडूनियां जर्जर जीर्ण वस्त्रें । मनुष्य घेतो दुसरी नवीन ॥ तशींच टाकूनि जुनीं शरीरें । आत्मा हि घेतो दुसरी निराळी
verses.roman: sāṇḍūniyāñ jarjar jīrṇ vastrēṁ । manuṣy ghētō dusarī navīn ॥ taśīñc ṭākūni junīṁ śarīrēṁ । ātmā hi ghētō dusarī nirāḷī
verses.summary: Krishna says as a person discards worn-out clothes for new ones, the soul casts off old bodies and takes on new ones.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: शस्त्रें न चिरिती ह्यास ह्यास अग्नी न जाळितो । पाणी न भिजवी ह्यास ह्यास वारा न वाळवी
verses.roman: śastrēn n ciritī hyās hyās agnī n jāḷitō । pāṇī n bhijavī hyās hyās vārā n vāḷavī
verses.summary: Krishna says weapons cannot cut the soul, fire cannot burn it, water cannot wet it, wind cannot dry it.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: चिरवे जाळवे ना हा भिजवे वाळवे हि ना । स्थिर निश्चळ हा नित्य सर्व व्यापी सनातन
verses.roman: ciravē jāḷavē nā hā bhijavē vāḷavē hi nā । sthir niścaḷ hā nity sarv vyāpī sanātan
verses.summary: Krishna says the soul cannot be cut, burned, wetted, or dried; it is steady, unmoving, eternal, all-pervading, everlasting.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: न देखूं ये न चिंतूं ये बोलिला निर्विकार हा जाणूनि ह्यापरी आत्मा शोक योग्य नसे तुज ।
verses.roman: n dēkhūṁ yē n cintūṁ yē bōlilā nirvikār hā jāṇūni hyāparī ātmā śōk yōgy nasē tuj ।
verses.summary: Krishna says the soul is called unseeable, unthinkable, and unchanging; knowing it thus, Arjuna shouldn't grieve.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अथवा पाहसी तूं हा मरे जन्मे प्रति-क्षणीं । तरी तुज कुठें येथें नसे शोकास कारण
verses.roman: athavā pāhasī tūṁ hā marē janmē prati-kṣaṇīṁ । tarī tuj kuṭhēṁ yēthēn nasē śōkās kāraṇ
verses.summary: Krishna says even if Arjuna thinks the soul is constantly born and dies, he still has no reason to grieve.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: जन्मतां निश्चयें मृत्यू मरतां जन्म निश्चयें । म्हणूनि न टळे त्याचा शोक व्यर्थ करूं नको
verses.roman: janmatān niścayēm mr̥tyū maratāñ janm niścayēṁ । mhaṇūni n ṭaḷē tyācā śōk vyarth karūn nakō
verses.summary: Krishna says death is certain for the born and birth for the dead; this is unavoidable, so don't grieve uselessly.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: भूतांचे मूळ अव्यक्तीं मध्य तो व्यक्त भासतो । पुन्हा शेवट अव्यक्तीं त्यामधें शोक कायसा
verses.roman: bhūtāñcē mūḷ avyaktīm madhy tō vyakt bhāsatō । punhā śēvaṭ avyaktīn tyāmadhēṁ śōk kāyasā
verses.summary: Krishna says beings are unmanifest before birth, manifest in between, and unmanifest again at death—why grieve over this?

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: आश्चर्य जो साच चि ह्यास देखे । आश्चर्य जो देखत ह्यास वर्णी ॥ आश्चर्य जो वर्णन तेंहि ऐके । ऐके तरी शून्यचि जाणण्याचें ।
verses.roman: āścary jō sāc ci hyās dēkhē । āścary jō dēkhat hyās varṇī ॥ āścary jō varṇan tēṁhi aikē । aikē tarī śūnyaci jāṇaṇyācēṁ ।
verses.summary: Krishna says one sees the soul as a wonder, another speaks of it as a wonder, another hears of it as a wonder, yet none truly knows it.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: वसे देहांत सर्वाच्या आत्मा अमर नित्य हा । म्हणून भूतमात्रीं तूं नको शोक करूं कधीं ॥
verses.roman: vasē dēhānt sarvācyā ātmā amar nity hā । mhaṇūn bhūtamātrīn tūn nakō śōk karūṅ kadhīṁ ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says the soul dwelling in all bodies is eternal and cannot be slain, so Arjuna should never grieve for any being.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: स्वधर्म तोहि पाहूनि न योग्य डगणें तुज । धर्मयुध्दाहुनी कांहीं क्षत्रियास नसे भलें
verses.roman: svadharm tōhi pāhūni n yōgy ḍagaṇēn tuj । dharmayudhdāhunī kāṁhīṅ kṣatriyās nasē bhalēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says considering his own duty Arjuna shouldn't waver; for a warrior nothing is better than a righteous battle.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: प्राप्त झालें अनायासें स्वर्गचें व्दार मोकळें क्षत्रियास महा-भाग्यें लाभतें युध्द हें असें
verses.roman: prāpt jhālēṁ anāyāsēṁ svargacēṁ vdār mōkaḷēṅ kṣatriyās mahā-bhāgyēṁ lābhatēṁ yudhd hēṁ asēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says such a battle, an open door to heaven, comes only to fortunate warriors.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: हें धर्मयध्द टाळूनि पापांत पडशील तूं । स्वधर्मासह कीर्तीस दूर सारुनियां स्वयें ।
verses.roman: hēn dharmayadhd ṭāḷūni pāpānt paḍaśīl tūṁ । svadharmāsah kīrtīs dūr sāruniyāṁ svayēṁ ।
verses.summary: Krishna says if Arjuna avoids this righteous war, he will incur sin and lose his duty and his fame.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अखंड लोक गातील दुष्कीर्ति जगतीं तुझी । मानवंतास दुष्कीर्ति मरणाहूनि आगळी
verses.roman: akhaṇḍ lōk gātīl duṣkīrti jagatīn tujhī । mānavantās duṣkīrti maraṇāhūni āgaḷī
verses.summary: Krishna says people will forever speak of Arjuna's disgrace, and for an honored man dishonor is worse than death.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: भिऊनि टाळिलें युध्द मानितील महा-रथी । असुनि मान्य त् ह्यांस तुच्छता पावशील कीं
verses.roman: bhiūni ṭāḷilēṁ yudhd mānitīl mahā-rathī । asuni māny t hyāṁs tucchatā pāvaśīl kīṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says the great warriors will think Arjuna fled from fear, and those who respected him will hold him in contempt.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: बोलितील तुझे शत्रु भलतें भलतें बहु । निंदितील तुझें शौर्य काय त्याहूनि दुःखद ।
verses.roman: bōlitīl tujhē śatru bhalatēm bhalatēm bahu । ninditīl tujhēṁ śaury kāy tyāhūni duḥkhad ।
verses.summary: Krishna says his enemies will speak many insults and mock his prowess—what could be more painful than that?

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: मेल्यानें भोगिसी स्वर्ग जिंकिल्यानें मही-तळ । म्हणुनि अर्जुना ऊठ युध्दास दृढ निश्चयें
verses.roman: mēlyānēm bhōgisī svarg jiṅkilyānēm mahī-taḷ । mhaṇuni arjunā ūṭh yudhdās dr̥ḍh niścayēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says if slain Arjuna gains heaven, if victorious he gains the earth; so he should rise with firm resolve to fight.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: हानि लाभ सुखें दुःखें हार जीत करी सम । मग युध्दास हो सिध्द न लागे पाप तें तुज ॥
verses.roman: hāni lābh sukhēn duḥkhēṁ hār jīt karī sam । mag yudhdās hō sidhd n lāgē pāp tēn tuj ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says treating loss and gain, pleasure and pain, victory and defeat alike, Arjuna should fight; then no sin will touch him.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: सांख्य-बुध्दी अशी जाण ऐक ती योग-बुध्दि तूं । तोडिशील जिनें सारीं कर्माची बंधनें जगीं
verses.roman: sāṅkhya-budhdī aśī jāṇ aik tī yōga-budhdi tūṁ । tōḍiśīl jinēṁ sārīṅ karmācī bandhanēñ jagīṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says he has explained the wisdom of Sankhya; now he will explain the wisdom of yoga, by which Arjuna can break the bonds of action.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: न बुडे येथ आरंभ न घडे विपरीत हि । जोडा स्वल्प हि हा धर्म तारी मोठया भयांतुनी
verses.roman: n buḍē yēth ārambh n ghaḍē viparīt hi । jōḍā svalp hi hā dharm tārī mōṭhayā bhayāntunī
verses.summary: Krishna says in this path no effort is wasted and nothing goes wrong; even a little of this practice saves one from great fear.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ह्यांत निश्चय लाभूनि बुध्दि एकाग्र राहते । निश्चयाविण बुध्दीचे फाटे ते संपती चि ना ।
verses.roman: hyānt niścay lābhūni budhdi ēkāgr rāhatē । niścayāviṇ budhdīcē phāṭē tē sampatī ci nā ।
verses.summary: Krishna says here the resolute mind stays one-pointed, while the irresolute mind branches off endlessly.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अविवेकी वृथा वाणी बोलती फुलवूनियां । वेदाचे घालिती वाद म्हणती दुसरें नसे
verses.roman: avivēkī vr̥thā vāṇī bōlatī phulavūniyāṁ । vēdācē ghālitī vād mhaṇatī dusarēn nasē
verses.summary: Krishna says the unwise utter flowery words, argue over the Vedas, and claim there is nothing beyond ritual.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: जन्मूनियां करा कर्में मिळवा भोग वैभव । भोगा कर्म-फळें गोड सांगती स्वर्ग-कामुक
verses.roman: janmūniyāṅ karā karmēm miḷavā bhōg vaibhav । bhōgā karma-phaḷēṅ gōḍ sāṅgatī svarga-kāmuk
verses.summary: Krishna says those craving heaven preach rebirth, enjoyment, and prosperity as the sweet fruits of ritual action.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: त्यामुळें भुलली बुध्दी गुंतली भोग वैभवीं । ती निश्चय न लाभूनि समाधींत नव्हे स्थिर ।
verses.roman: tyāmuḷēm bhulalī budhdī guntalī bhōg vaibhavīṁ । tī niścay n lābhūni samādhīnt navhē sthir ।
verses.summary: Krishna says minds attached to enjoyment and prosperity are deluded and cannot become steady in meditation.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: तिन्ही गुण वदे वेद त्यांत राहें अलिप्त तूं । सत्व सोडूं नको सोशी द्वंद्वें निश्चिंत सावध
verses.roman: tinhī guṇ vadē vēd tyānt rāhēṁ alipt tūṁ । satv sōḍūn nakō sōśī dvandvēn niścint sāvadh
verses.summary: Krishna says the Vedas deal with the three gunas; Arjuna should rise above them, hold to goodness, and endure the dualities, alert and self-possessed.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: सर्वत्र भरलें पाणी तेव्हां आडांत अर्थ जो । विज्ञानी ब्रह्मवेत्यास सर्व वेदांत अर्थ तो ॥
verses.roman: sarvatr bharalēm pāṇī tēvhāṁ āḍānt arth jō । vijñānī brahmavētyās sarv vēdānt arth tō ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says just as a well's use is small where water is everywhere, so all the Vedas' value is contained for the knower of Brahman.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: कर्मांत चि तुझा भाग तो फळांत नसो कधीं । नको कर्म-फळीं हेतु अकर्मीं वासना नको
verses.roman: karmānt ci tujhā bhāg tō phaḷānt nasō kadhīṁ । nakō karma-phaḷīṁ hētu akarmīṁ vāsanā nakō
verses.summary: Krishna says Arjuna's right is to action alone, never to its fruits; he should not act for results nor be attached to inaction.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: फळ लाभो न लाभो तू निःसंग होउनी । योग-युक्त करी कर्मे योग-सार समत्व चि
verses.roman: phaḷ lābhō n lābhō tū niḥsaṅg hōunī । yōga-yukt karī karmē yōga-sār samatv ci
verses.summary: Krishna says perform action unattached, indifferent to success or failure; this evenness of mind is the essence of yoga.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: समत्व-बुध्दि ही थोर कर्म तीहुनि हीन चि । बुध्दीचा आसरा घे तूं मागती फळ दीन ते
verses.roman: samatva-budhdi hī thōr karm tīhuni hīn ci । budhdīcā āsarā ghē tūm māgatī phaḷ dīn tē
verses.summary: Krishna says action is far inferior to the yoga of evenness; take refuge in this wisdom, for those who seek fruits are wretched.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: येथें समत्व बुध्दीनें टळे सुकृत-दुष्कृत । समत्व जोड ह्यासाठीं तें चि कर्मांत कौशल
verses.roman: yēthēṁ samatv budhdīnēṇ ṭaḷē sukr̥ta-duṣkr̥t । samatv jōḍ hyāsāṭhīn tēñ ci karmānt kauśal
verses.summary: Krishna says one with even-minded wisdom escapes both good and bad deeds; so cultivate this evenness, which is skill in action.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ज्ञानी समत्व-बुध्दीने कर्माचें फळ सोडुनी । जन्माचे तोडिती बंध पावती पद अच्युत
verses.roman: jñānī samatva-budhdīnē karmācēm phaḷ sōḍunī । janmācē tōḍitī bandh pāvatī pad acyut
verses.summary: Krishna says the wise, renouncing the fruits of action through even-minded wisdom, break the bonds of birth and reach the imperishable state.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: लंघूनि बुध्दि जाईल जेव्हां हा मोह कर्दम । आलें येईल जें कानी तेव्हां जिरविशील तूं
verses.roman: laṅghūni budhdi jāīl jēvhāṁ hā mōh kardam । ālēṁ yēīl jēṅ kānī tēvhāñ jiraviśīl tūṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says when Arjuna's intellect crosses beyond the mire of delusion, he will become indifferent to all that he has heard and will hear.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: श्रवणें भ्रमली बुध्दि तुझी लाभूनि निश्चय । स्थिरावेल समधींत तेव्हां भेटेल योग तो ॥
verses.roman: śravaṇēm bhramalī budhdi tujhī lābhūni niścay । sthirāvēl samadhīnt tēvhām bhēṭēl yōg tō ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says when his intellect, confused by scripture, becomes steady and fixed in meditation, then he will attain yoga.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: स्थिरावला समाधींत स्थित-प्रज्ञ कसा असे । कृष्णा सांग कसा बोले कसा राहे फिरे कसा
verses.roman: sthirāvalā samādhīnt sthita-prajñ kasā asē । kr̥ṣṇā sāṅg kasā bōlē kasā rāhē phirē kasā
verses.summary: Arjuna asks Krishna how the person of steady wisdom is described—how he speaks, sits, and moves.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: कामना अंतरांतील सर्व सोडूनि जो स्वयें । आत्म्यांत चि असे तुष्ट तो स्थित-प्रज्ञ बोलिला
verses.roman: kāmanā antarāntīl sarv sōḍūni jō svayēṁ । ātmyānt ci asē tuṣṭ tō sthita-prajñ bōlilā
verses.summary: Krishna says one who abandons all desires of the mind and is content within the self is called a person of steady wisdom.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: नस दुःखांत उद्वेग सुखाची लालसा नसे । नसे तृष्णा भय क्रोध तो स्थित-प्रज्ञ संयमी
verses.roman: nas duḥkhānt udvēg sukhācī lālasā nasē । nasē tr̥ṣṇā bhay krōdh tō sthita-prajñ saṁyamī
verses.summary: Krishna says one undisturbed by sorrow, free of craving for pleasure, and without longing, fear, or anger is a steady-minded sage.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: सर्वत्र जो अनासक्त बरें वाईट लाभतां । न उल्लासे न संतापे त्याची प्रज्ञा स्थिरावली
verses.roman: sarvatr jō anāsakt barēṁ vāīṭ lābhatāṁ । n ullāsē n santāpē tyācī prajñā sthirāvalī
verses.summary: Krishna says one unattached everywhere, neither delighting in good nor recoiling from bad, has steady wisdom.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: घेई ओढूनि संपूर्ण विषयांतूनि इंद्रियें । जसा कांसव तो अंगें तेव्हां प्रज्ञा स्थिरावली।
verses.roman: ghēī ōḍhūni sampūrṇ viṣayāntūni indriyēṁ । jasā kāṁsav tō aṅgēn tēvhām prajñā sthirāvalī।
verses.summary: Krishna says when one withdraws the senses from their objects like a tortoise drawing in its limbs, his wisdom is steady.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: निराहार-बळें बाह्य सोडी विषय साधक । आंतील न सुटे गोडी ती सुटे आत्म-दर्शनें
verses.roman: nirāhāra-baḷēm bāhy sōḍī viṣay sādhak । āntīl n suṭē gōḍī tī suṭē ātma-darśanēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says abstinence makes outer objects fall away but the inner taste remains; it vanishes only on seeing the supreme.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: करीत असतां प्रयत्न ज्ञात्याच्या हि मनास ही । नेती खेंचूनि वेगानें इंद्रियें दांडगीं चि कीं
verses.roman: karīt asatām prayatn jñātyācyā hi manās hī । nētī khēñcūni vēgānēṁ indriyēn dāṇḍagīñ ci kīṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says even a wise, striving person's mind can be forcibly carried away by the turbulent senses.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: त्यांस रोधूनि युक्तीनें रहावें मत्परायण । इंद्रियें जिंकिली ज्यानें त्याची प्रज्ञा स्थिरावली
verses.roman: tyāṁs rōdhūni yuktīnēṁ rahāvēm matparāyaṇ । indriyēñ jiṅkilī jyānēn tyācī prajñā sthirāvalī
verses.summary: Krishna says one should restrain the senses skillfully and remain devoted to him; one who masters the senses has steady wisdom.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: विषयांचें करी ध्यान त्यास तो संग लागला संगांतूनि फुटे काम क्रोध कामांत ठेविला
verses.roman: viṣayāñcēṅ karī dhyān tyās tō saṅg lāgalā saṅgāntūni phuṭē kām krōdh kāmānt ṭhēvilā
verses.summary: Krishna says dwelling on sense objects breeds attachment, attachment breeds desire, and desire breeds anger.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: क्रोधांतूनि जडे मोह मोहानें स्मृति लोपली । स्मृति-लोपें बुध्दि-नाश म्हणजे आत्म-नाश चि
verses.roman: krōdhāntūni jaḍē mōh mōhānēṁ smr̥ti lōpalī । smr̥ti-lōpēm budhdi-nāś mhaṇajē ātma-nāś ci
verses.summary: Krishna says anger causes delusion, delusion ruins memory, loss of memory destroys the intellect, and that is self-destruction.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: राग व्देश परी जातां आलीं हातांत इंद्रियें । स्वामित्वें विषयीं वर्ते त्यास लाभे पसन्नता
verses.roman: rāg vdēś parī jātāṁ ālīṁ hātānt indriyēṁ । svāmitvēṁ viṣayīṁ vartē tyās lābhē pasannatā
verses.summary: Krishna says one who moves among objects with senses under control, free of attraction and aversion, attains serenity.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: प्रसन्नतेपुढें सर्व दुःखें जाती झडूनियां । प्रसन्नतेने बुध्दीची स्थिरता शीघ्र होतसे
verses.roman: prasannatēpuḍhēṁ sarv duḥkhēñ jātī jhaḍūniyāṁ । prasannatēnē budhdīcī sthiratā śīghr hōtasē
verses.summary: Krishna says in serenity all sorrows fall away, and the serene person's intellect quickly becomes steady.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अयुक्तास नसे बुध्दि त्यामुळें भावना नसे । म्हणूनि न मिळे शांति शांतीविण कसें सुख
verses.roman: ayuktās nasē budhdi tyāmuḷēm bhāvanā nasē । mhaṇūni n miḷē śānti śāntīviṇ kasēṁ sukh
verses.summary: Krishna says the undisciplined has no steady intellect or contemplation, hence no peace; and without peace, how can there be happiness?

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: इंद्रियें वर्ततां स्वैर त्यामागें मन जाय जें । त्यानें प्रज्ञा जशी नौका वान्यानें खेंचली जळीं
verses.roman: indriyēṁ vartatāṁ svair tyāmāgēm man jāy jēṁ । tyānēm prajñā jaśī naukā vānyānēṅ khēñcalī jaḷīṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says when the mind follows the wandering senses, it carries away wisdom like wind carrying a boat on water.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: म्हणूनि इंद्रियें ज्यानें विषयांतूनि सर्वथा । ओढूनि घेतलीं आंत त्याची प्रज्ञा स्थिरावली ।
verses.roman: mhaṇūni indriyēñ jyānēṁ viṣayāntūni sarvathā । ōḍhūni ghētalīṁ ānt tyācī prajñā sthirāvalī ।
verses.summary: Krishna says therefore one who fully withdraws the senses from their objects has steady wisdom.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: सर्व भूतांस जी रात्र जागतो संयमी तिथें । सर्व भूतें जिथें जागीं ज्ञानी योग्यास रात्र ती
verses.roman: sarv bhūtāṁs jī rātr jāgatō saṁyamī tithēṁ । sarv bhūtēñ jithēñ jāgīñ jñānī yōgyās rātr tī
verses.summary: Krishna says what is night for all beings is the waking time of the disciplined sage, and what beings are awake to is night for the seer.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: न भंग पावे भरतां हि नित्य । समुद्र घेतो जिरवूनि पाणी । जाती तसे ज्यांत जिरुनि भोग । तो पावला शांति न भोग-लुब्ध
verses.roman: n bhaṅg pāvē bharatāṁ hi nity । samudr ghētō jiravūni pāṇī । jātī tasē jyānt jiruni bhōg । tō pāvalā śānti n bhōga-lubdh
verses.summary: Krishna says like the ocean that stays unmoved as waters pour in, one in whom desires dissolve attains peace—not the desire-driven.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: सोडूनि कामना सर्व फिरे होऊनि निस्पृह । अहंता ममता गेली झाला तो शांति-रुप चि ।
verses.roman: sōḍūni kāmanā sarv phirē hōūni nispr̥h । ahantā mamatā gēlī jhālā tō śānti-rup ci ।
verses.summary: Krishna says one who abandons all desires and lives free of craving, ego, and possessiveness becomes peace itself.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अर्जुना स्थिति ही ब्राह्मी पावतां न चळे पुन्हां । टिकूनि अंत-काळीं हि ब्रह्म-निर्वाण मेळवी
verses.roman: arjunā sthiti hī brāhmī pāvatān n caḷē punhāṁ । ṭikūni anta-kāḷīṁ hi brahma-nirvāṇ mēḷavī
verses.summary: Krishna says this is the state of Brahman; attaining it one is not deluded again, and abiding in it even at death, one reaches liberation in Brahman.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: बुध्दि कर्माहुनी थोर मानिसी तूं जनार्दना । मग कर्मांत कां घोर घालिसी मज केशवा
verses.roman: budhdi karmāhunī thōr mānisī tūñ janārdanā । mag karmānt kāṅ ghōr ghālisī maj kēśavā
verses.summary: Arjuna asks Krishna: if you hold knowledge superior to action, why then push me into this terrible action?

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: मिश्र बोलूनि बुध्दीस जणूं मोहांत टाकिसी । ज्यानें मी श्रेय पावेन सांग तें एक निश्चित ।
verses.roman: miśr bōlūni budhdīs jaṇūm mōhānt ṭākisī । jyānēm mī śrēy pāvēn sāṅg tēṁ ēk niścit ।
verses.summary: Arjuna says Krishna's mixed words confuse his mind; he asks for one definite path that will lead to his good.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: दुहेरी या जगीं निष्ठा पूर्वीं मीं बोलिलों असे । ज्ञानानें सांख्य जी पावे योगी कर्म करुनियां
verses.roman: duhērī yā jagīn niṣṭhā pūrvīm mīm bōlilōṁ asē । jñānānēṁ sāṅkhy jī pāvē yōgī karm karuniyāṁ
verses.summary: Krishna replies that he earlier described two paths: knowledge-yoga for the Sankhyas and action-yoga for the doers.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: न कर्मारंभ टाळूनि लाभे नैष्कर्म्य तें कधीं । संन्यासाच्या क्रियेने चि कोणी सिध्दि न मेळवी
verses.roman: n karmārambh ṭāḷūni lābhē naiṣkarmy tēṅ kadhīṁ । sannyāsācyā kriyēnē ci kōṇī sidhdi n mēḷavī
verses.summary: Krishna says one cannot attain freedom from action merely by avoiding action, nor reach perfection by mere renunciation.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: कर्माविण कधीं कोणी न राहे क्षण-मात्र हि । प्रकृतीच्या गुणीं सार बांधिले करितात चि ।
verses.roman: karmāviṇ kadhīṅ kōṇī n rāhē kṣaṇa-mātr hi । prakr̥tīcyā guṇīṁ sār bāndhilē karitāt ci ।
verses.summary: Krishna notes no one can stay without acting even a moment; everyone is driven to act by nature's qualities.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: इंद्रियें करिती कर्म मूढ त्यांस चि रोधुनी । राहतो भोग चिंतूनि तो मिथ्याचार बोलिला
verses.roman: indriyēṅ karitī karm mūḍh tyāṁs ci rōdhunī । rāhatō bhōg cintūni tō mithyācār bōlilā
verses.summary: Krishna calls one a hypocrite who restrains the senses outwardly while dwelling on sense-objects in the mind.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: जो इंद्रियें मनानें तीं नमुनी त्यांस राबवी कर्म योगांत निःसंग तो विशेष चि मानिला
verses.roman: jō indriyēm manānēn tīn namunī tyāṁs rābavī karm yōgānt niḥsaṅg tō viśēṣ ci mānilā
verses.summary: Krishna praises the one who controls the senses with the mind and engages them in detached action.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: नेमिलें तूं करी कर्म करणें हें चि थोर कीं । तुझी शरीर यात्रा ही कर्माविण घडे चि ना ॥
verses.roman: nēmilēn tūṅ karī karm karaṇēṁ hēñ ci thōr kīṁ । tujhī śarīr yātrā hī karmāviṇ ghaḍē ci nā ॥
verses.summary: Krishna tells Arjuna to do his prescribed duty; action is better than inaction, and even bodily life needs action.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: यज्ञार्थ कर्म सोडूनि लोक हा बांधिला असे । यज्ञार्थ आचरीं कर्म अर्जुना मुक्त-संग तूं ।
verses.roman: yajñārth karm sōḍūni lōk hā bāndhilā asē । yajñārth ācarīṅ karm arjunā mukta-saṅg tūṁ ।
verses.summary: Krishna says action done for sacrifice frees one; do your work as sacrifice, free from attachment.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: पूर्वीं प्रजेसवें ब्रह्मा यज्ञ निर्मूनि बोलिला । पावा उत्कर्ष यज्ञानें हा तुम्हां काम-धेनु चि
verses.roman: pūrvīm prajēsavēm brahmā yajñ nirmūni bōlilā । pāvā utkarṣ yajñānēṁ hā tumhāṅ kāma-dhēnu ci
verses.summary: Krishna recalls that the creator established sacrifice along with mankind, saying it would fulfill their wishes like a wish-cow.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: रक्षा देवांस यज्ञानें तुम्हां रक्षोत देव ते । एकमेकांस रक्षूनि पावा कल्याण सर्व हि
verses.roman: rakṣā dēvāṁs yajñānēn tumhāṁ rakṣōt dēv tē । ēkamēkāṁs rakṣūni pāvā kalyāṇ sarv hi
verses.summary: Krishna says nourish the gods through sacrifice and they nourish you; by mutual support all attain welfare.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: यज्ञ-तुष्ट तुम्हां देव भोग देतील वांछित । त्यांचें त्यांस न देतां जो खाय तो एक चोर चि ।
verses.roman: yajña-tuṣṭ tumhān dēv bhōg dētīl vāñchit । tyāñcēn tyāṁs n dētāñ jō khāy tō ēk cōr ci ।
verses.summary: Krishna says the pleased gods grant desired enjoyments; one who enjoys without offering back is a thief.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: यज्ञांत उरलें खाती संत ते दोष जाळिती । रांधिती आपुल्यासाठीं पापी ते पाप भक्षिती ॥
verses.roman: yajñānt uralēṅ khātī sant tē dōṣ jāḷitī । rāndhitī āpulyāsāṭhīm pāpī tē pāp bhakṣitī ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says the good who eat sacrifice's remnants are freed from fault, while those who cook only for themselves consume sin.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अन्नापासूनि ही भूतें पर्जन्यांतूनि अन्न तें । यज्ञें पर्जन्य तो होय यज्ञ कर्मामुळें घडे
verses.roman: annāpāsūni hī bhūtēm parjanyāntūni ann tēṁ । yajñēm parjany tō hōy yajñ karmāmuḷēṅ ghaḍē
verses.summary: Krishna traces the cycle: beings from food, food from rain, rain from sacrifice, sacrifice from action.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: प्रकृतीपासूनि कर्म ब्रह्मीं प्रकृति राहिली । ऐसें व्यापक तें ब्रह्म यज्ञांत भरलें सदा
verses.roman: prakr̥tīpāsūni karm brahmīm prakr̥ti rāhilī । aisēṁ vyāpak tēm brahm yajñānt bharalēṁ sadā
verses.summary: Krishna says action arises from nature and nature rests in Brahman; the all-pervading Brahman is ever present in sacrifice.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: प्रेरिलें हें असें चक्र ह्या लोकीं जो न चालवी । इंद्रियासक्त तो पापी व्यर्थ जीवन घालवी ॥
verses.roman: prērilēṁ hēṁ asēñ cakr hyā lōkīñ jō n cālavī । indriyāsakt tō pāpī vyarth jīvan ghālavī ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says one who doesn't keep this set wheel turning lives in vain, indulging the senses sinfully.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: परी आत्म्यांत जो खेळे आत्मा भोगूनि तृप्त जा । आत्म्यामधें चि संतुष्ट त्याचें कर्तव्य संपलें
verses.roman: parī ātmyānt jō khēḷē ātmā bhōgūni tr̥pt jā । ātmyāmadhēñ ci santuṣṭ tyācēṅ kartavy sampalēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says the one who delights in the Self, content within the Self, has no duty left to perform.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: केल्यानें वा न केल्यानें त्यास भावार्थ सारखा । कोणामधें कुठें त्याचा न कांहीं लोभ गुंतला ।
verses.roman: kēlyānēṁ vā n kēlyānēn tyās bhāvārth sārakhā । kōṇāmadhēṅ kuṭhēn tyācā n kāṁhīṁ lōbh guntalā ।
verses.summary: Krishna says such a person gains nothing by doing or not doing, and depends on no being for any interest.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: म्हणूनि नित्य निःसंग करी कर्तव्य कर्म तूं । निःसंग करितां कर्म कैवल्य-पद पावतो ॥
verses.roman: mhaṇūni nity niḥsaṅg karī kartavy karm tūṁ । niḥsaṅg karitāṅ karm kaivalya-pad pāvatō ॥
verses.summary: Krishna therefore tells Arjuna to do his duty without attachment; acting detached, one attains liberation.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: कर्म-व्दारा चि सिध्दीस पावले जनकादिक । करी तू कर्म लक्षूनि लोक-संग्रह धर्म हि
verses.roman: karma-vdārā ci sidhdīs pāvalē janakādik । karī tū karm lakṣūni lōka-saṅgrah dharm hi
verses.summary: Krishna says Janaka and others reached perfection through action; act for the sake of guiding people too.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: जें जें आचरितो श्रेष्ठ तें तें चि दुसरे जन । तो मान्य करितो जें जें लोक चालवितात तें ।
verses.roman: jēñ jēṁ ācaritō śrēṣṭh tēn tēñ ci dusarē jan । tō māny karitō jēñ jēṁ lōk cālavitāt tēṁ ।
verses.summary: Krishna says whatever a great person does, others follow; people adopt whatever standard he sets.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: करावें-मिळवावेंसें नसे कांहीं जरी मज । तिन्ही लोकीं तरी पार्था कर्मीं मी वागतो चि कीं
verses.roman: karāvēṁ-miḷavāvēṁsēn nasē kāṁhīñ jarī maj । tinhī lōkīn tarī pārthā karmīm mī vāgatō ci kīṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says though he has nothing to gain or attain in the three worlds, he still engages in action.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: मी चि कर्मीं न वागन जरी आळस झाडुनी । सर्वथा लोक घेतील माझें वर्तन तें मग
verses.roman: mī ci karmīn n vāgan jarī āḷas jhāḍunī । sarvathā lōk ghētīl mājhēṁ vartan tēm mag
verses.summary: Krishna says if he stopped acting diligently, people everywhere would imitate his example.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: सोडीन मी जरी कर्म नष्ट होतील लोक हे । होईन संकर-व्दारा मी चि घातास कारण
verses.roman: sōḍīn mī jarī karm naṣṭ hōtīl lōk hē । hōīn saṅkara-vdārā mī ci ghātās kāraṇ
verses.summary: Krishna says if he abandoned action these worlds would perish and he would cause confusion and ruin.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: गूंतूनि करिती अज्ञ ज्ञात्यानें मोकळेपणें । करावें कर्म तैसें चि इच्छुनी लोक-संग्रह
verses.roman: gūntūni karitī ajñ jñātyānēm mōkaḷēpaṇēṁ । karāvēṅ karm taisēñ ci icchunī lōka-saṅgrah
verses.summary: Krishna says the wise should act, like the ignorant who act with attachment, but freely, wishing to guide people.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: नेणत्या कर्म-निष्ठांचा बुध्दि-भेद करुं नये । गोडी कर्मांत लावावी समत्वें आचरूनि तीं
verses.roman: nēṇatyā karma-niṣṭhāñcā budhdi-bhēd karun nayē । gōḍī karmānt lāvāvī samatvēṁ ācarūni tīṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says the wise should not unsettle the minds of the ignorant attached to action, but inspire love for action by doing it evenly.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: कर्में होतात हीं सारी प्रकृतीच्या गुणांमुळें । अहंकार-बळें मूढ कर्ता मी हें चि घेतसे
verses.roman: karmēṁ hōtāt hīṁ sārī prakr̥tīcyā guṇāmmuḷēṁ । ahaṅkāra-baḷēm mūḍh kartā mī hēñ ci ghētasē
verses.summary: Krishna says all actions are done by nature's qualities; the deluded ego thinks 'I am the doer'.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: गुण हे आणि हीं कर्में ह्यांहुनी वेगळा चि मी । जाणे तत्व-ज्ञ गुंते ना गुणांत गुण वागतां
verses.roman: guṇ hē āṇi hīṅ karmēṁ hyāṁhunī vēgaḷā ci mī । jāṇē tatva-jñ guntē nā guṇānt guṇ vāgatāṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says one who knows the truth—that qualities act among qualities, separate from himself—does not get entangled.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: घेतले गुण-कर्मीं जे भुलले प्रकृती-गुणें । त्या अल्प जाणणारांस सर्व-ज्ञें चाळवूं नये
verses.roman: ghētalē guṇa-karmīñ jē bhulalē prakr̥tī-guṇēṁ । tyā alp jāṇaṇārāṁs sarva-jñēñ cāḷavūn nayē
verses.summary: Krishna says those deluded by nature's qualities cling to actions; the wise should not disturb the partly-knowing.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: मज अध्यात्म-वृत्तीनें सर्व कर्में समर्पुनी फलाशा ममता सर्व सोडुनी झुंज तूं सुखें
verses.roman: maj adhyātma-vr̥ttīnēṁ sarv karmēṁ samarpunī phalāśā mamatā sarv sōḍunī jhuñj tūṁ sukhēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna tells Arjuna to dedicate all actions to him with spiritual awareness, abandon longing for fruit and possessiveness, and fight at ease.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: माझें शासन हें नित्य जे निर्मत्सर पाळिती । श्रध्देने नेणते ते हि तोडिती कर्म-बंधनें
verses.roman: mājhēṁ śāsan hēn nity jē nirmatsar pāḷitī । śradhdēnē nēṇatē tē hi tōḍitī karma-bandhanēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says those who without envy follow his teaching with faith are freed from the bondage of action.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: परी मत्सर-बुध्दीने जे हें शसन मोडिती । ज्ञान-शून्य चि ते मूढ पावले नाश जाण तूं।
verses.roman: parī matsara-budhdīnē jē hēṁ śasan mōḍitī । jñāna-śūny ci tē mūḍh pāvalē nāś jāṇ tūṁ।
verses.summary: Krishna says those who reject his teaching out of envy are deluded, devoid of knowledge, and doomed.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ज्ञानी हि वागतो त्याच्या स्वभावास धरूनियां । स्वभाव-वश ही भूतें बलात्कार निरर्थक
verses.roman: jñānī hi vāgatō tyācyā svabhāvās dharūniyāṁ । svabhāva-vaś hī bhūtēm balātkār nirarthak
verses.summary: Krishna says even the wise act according to their nature; beings follow their nature, so force is useless.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: इंद्रियीं सेवितां अर्थ राग-व्देश उभे तिथें । वश होऊं नये त्यांस ते मार्गातील चोर चि
verses.roman: indriyīṁ sēvitāṁ arth rāga-vdēś ubhē tithēṁ । vaś hōūn nayē tyāṁs tē mārgātīl cōr ci
verses.summary: Krishna warns that attraction and aversion lurk in sense-objects; don't be controlled by them, for they are robbers on the path.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: उणा हि आपुला धर्म पर-धर्माहुनी बरा । स्व-धर्मांत बरा मृत्यू पर-धर्म भयंकर
verses.roman: uṇā hi āpulā dharm para-dharmāhunī barā । sva-dharmānt barā mr̥tyū para-dharm bhayaṅkar
verses.summary: Krishna says one's own duty, though flawed, is better than another's done well; better to die in one's own duty.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: मनुष्य करितो पाप कोणाच्या प्रेरणेमुळे । आपुली नसतां इच्छा वेठीस धरला जसा।
verses.roman: manuṣy karitō pāp kōṇācyā prēraṇēmuḷē । āpulī nasatāṁ icchā vēṭhīs dharalā jasā।
verses.summary: Arjuna asks: what compels a person to commit sin, even unwillingly, as if forced?

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: काम हा आणि हा क्रोध घडिला जो रजोगुणें । मोठा खादाड पापिष्ठ तो वैरी जाण तूं इथें ।
verses.roman: kām hā āṇi hā krōdh ghaḍilā jō rajōguṇēṁ । mōṭhā khādāḍ pāpiṣṭh tō vairī jāṇ tūṁ ithēṁ ।
verses.summary: Krishna answers that desire and anger, born of the passion-quality, are the great devouring, sinful enemy here.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: धुरानें झांकिला अग्नी धुळीनें आरसा जसा । वारेनें वेष्टिला गर्भ कामानें ज्ञान हें तसें
verses.roman: dhurānēñ jhāṅkilā agnī dhuḷīnēṁ ārasā jasā । vārēnēṁ vēṣṭilā garbh kāmānēñ jñān hēn tasēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says knowledge is covered by desire as fire by smoke, a mirror by dust, an embryo by the womb.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: काम-रुप महा-अग्नि नव्हे तृप्त कधीं चि जो । जाणत्याचा सदा वैरी त्यानें हें ज्ञान झांकिलें
verses.roman: kāma-rup mahā-agni navhē tr̥pt kadhīñ ci jō । jāṇatyācā sadā vairī tyānēṁ hēñ jñān jhāṅkilēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says desire is an insatiable fire, the constant enemy of the wise, that veils knowledge.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: घेऊनि आसन्यासाठीं इंद्रियें मन बुद्धि तो । मोह पाडी मनुष्यातें त्याच्या ज्ञानास गुंडुनी
verses.roman: ghēūni āsanyāsāṭhīṁ indriyēm man buddhi tō । mōh pāḍī manuṣyātēn tyācyā jñānās guṇḍunī
verses.summary: Krishna says desire seats itself in the senses, mind, and intellect, deluding a person by covering his knowledge.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: म्हणूनि पहिला थारा इंद्रियें ती चि जिंकुनी । टाळी पाप्यास जो नाशी ज्ञान विज्ञान सर्व हि ।
verses.roman: mhaṇūni pahilā thārā indriyēn tī ci jiṅkunī । ṭāḷī pāpyās jō nāśī jñān vijñān sarv hi ।
verses.summary: Krishna says therefore first conquer the senses and slay this sinful desire that destroys knowledge and wisdom.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: इंद्रियें बोलिली थोर मन त्याहूनि थोर तें । बुद्धि थोर मनाहूनि थोर तीहूनि तो प्रभू
verses.roman: indriyēm bōlilī thōr man tyāhūni thōr tēṁ । buddhi thōr manāhūni thōr tīhūni tō prabhū
verses.summary: Krishna ranks them: senses high, mind higher, intellect above mind, and the Self beyond intellect.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: असा तो प्रभू जाणूनि आवरी आप आपणा । संहारी काम हा वैरी तूं गांठूनि परोपरीं ॥
verses.roman: asā tō prabhū jāṇūni āvarī āp āpaṇā । saṁhārī kām hā vairī tūṅ gāṇṭhūni parōparīṁ ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says, knowing the Self thus, steady yourself with yourself and slay this enemy desire, hard to grasp.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: योग हा अविनाशी मीं स्वयें सूर्यास बोलिलों । मनूस बोलिला सूर्य तो इक्ष्वाकूस त्यापरी
verses.roman: yōg hā avināśī mīṁ svayēṁ sūryās bōlilōṁ । manūs bōlilā sūry tō ikṣvākūs tyāparī
verses.summary: Krishna says he taught this imperishable yoga first to the sun, who passed it to Manu, who gave it to Ikshvaku.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अशा परंपरेतूनि हा राजर्षींस लाभला । पुढें काळ-बळानें तो ह्या लोकीं योग लोपला
verses.roman: aśā paramparētūni hā rājarṣīṁs lābhalā । puḍhēṅ kāḷa-baḷānēn tō hyā lōkīṁ yōg lōpalā
verses.summary: Krishna says the royal sages received it through this succession, but over time the yoga was lost in the world.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: तोच हा बोलिलों आज तुज योग पुरातन । जीवींचें गुज हें थोर तूं हि भक्त सखा तसा ॥
verses.roman: tōc hā bōlilōṁ āj tuj yōg purātan । jīvīñcēṅ guj hēn thōr tūṁ hi bhakt sakhā tasā ॥
verses.summary: Krishna tells Arjuna he is teaching that same ancient yoga today because Arjuna is his devotee and friend; it is a great secret.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: ह्या काळचा तुझा जन्म सूर्याचा तो पुरातन । तू बोलिलास आरंभी हें मीं जाण् कसें बरें ।
verses.roman: hyā kāḷacā tujhā janm sūryācā tō purātan । tū bōlilās ārambhī hēm mīñ jāṇ kasēm barēṁ ।
verses.summary: Arjuna asks how Krishna, born recently, could have taught this to the sun who lived long ago.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: माझे अनेक ह्यापूर्वी झले जन्म तसे तुझे । जाणतों सगळे मी ते अर्जुना तूं न जाणसी ।
verses.roman: mājhē anēk hyāpūrvī jhalē janm tasē tujhē । jāṇatōṁ sagaḷē mī tē arjunā tūn n jāṇasī ।
verses.summary: Krishna says both he and Arjuna have had many past births; he remembers them all, but Arjuna does not.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: असूनि हि अजन्मा मी निर्विकार जगत्-प्रभू । माझी प्रकृति वेढूनि मायेनें जन्मतों जणूं
verses.roman: asūni hi ajanmā mī nirvikār jagat-prabhū । mājhī prakr̥ti vēḍhūni māyēnēñ janmatōñ jaṇūṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says though he is unborn and changeless, lord of all, he takes birth through his own power over his nature.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: गळूनि जातसे धर्म ज्या ज्या वेळेस अर्जुना । अधर्म उठतो भारी तेव्हां मी जन्म घेतसे
verses.roman: gaḷūni jātasē dharm jyā jyā vēḷēs arjunā । adharm uṭhatō bhārī tēvhām mī janm ghētasē
verses.summary: Krishna says whenever righteousness declines and unrighteousness rises, he takes birth.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: राखावया जगीं संतां दुष्टां दूर करावया । स्थापावया पुन्हां धर्म जन्मतों मी युगीं युगीं ।
verses.roman: rākhāvayā jagīṁ santān duṣṭān dūr karāvayā । sthāpāvayā punhān dharm janmatōm mī yugīṁ yugīṁ ।
verses.summary: Krishna says he is born age after age to protect the good, destroy the wicked, and re-establish righteousness.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: जन्म कर्में अशीं दिव्य जो माझीं नीट ओळखे । देह गेल्या पुन्हा जन्म न पावे भेटुनी मज
verses.roman: janm karmēṁ aśīn divy jō mājhīn nīṭ ōḷakhē । dēh gēlyā punhā janm n pāvē bhēṭunī maj
verses.summary: Krishna says one who truly knows his divine birth and deeds is not reborn after death but comes to him.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: नाहीं तृष्णा भय क्रोध माझ्या सेवेंत तन्मय । झालें ज्ञान-तपें चोख अनेक मज पावले ॥
verses.roman: nāhīn tr̥ṣṇā bhay krōdh mājhyā sēvēnt tanmay । jhālēñ jñāna-tapēñ cōkh anēk maj pāvalē ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says many, freed of craving, fear and anger, absorbed in his service and purified by knowledge and austerity, have attained him.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: भजती जैसे जे मज भजे तैसाचि त्यांस मी । माझ्या मार्गास जे येती लोक कोणीकडूनि हि
verses.roman: bhajatī jaisē jē maj bhajē taisāci tyāṁs mī । mājhyā mārgās jē yētī lōk kōṇīkaḍūni hi
verses.summary: Krishna says he responds to people just as they approach him; all paths people follow lead to him.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: जे कर्म-सिद्धि वांछूनि यजिती येथ दैवतें । मनुष्य-लोकीं कर्माचें पावती फळ शीघ्र ते
verses.roman: jē karma-siddhi vāñchūni yajitī yēth daivatēṁ । manuṣya-lōkīṅ karmācēm pāvatī phaḷ śīghr tē
verses.summary: Krishna says those who want quick success in action worship the gods and gain swift results in the human world.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: निर्मिले वर्ण मी चारी गुण-कर्में विभागुनी । करुनि सर्व हें जाण अकर्ता निर्विकार मी ।
verses.roman: nirmilē varṇ mī cārī guṇa-karmēṁ vibhāgunī । karuni sarv hēñ jāṇ akartā nirvikār mī ।
verses.summary: Krishna says he created the four classes by dividing qualities and actions; though their maker, he is a non-doer and changeless.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: कर्में न बांधिती मातें फळीं इच्छा नसे मज । माझें स्वरुप हें जाणे तो कर्मांत हि मोकळा
verses.roman: karmēn n bāndhitī mātēm phaḷīṁ icchā nasē maj । mājhēṁ svarup hēñ jāṇē tō karmānt hi mōkaḷā
verses.summary: Krishna says actions do not bind him since he has no desire for their fruit; one who knows this is also free amid action.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: केलीं कर्में मुमुक्षूंनीं पूर्वी हें तत्व जाणुनी । तैसीं तू हि करी कर्में त्यांचा तो घेउनी धडा ॥
verses.roman: kēlīṅ karmēm mumukṣūnnīm pūrvī hēn tatv jāṇunī । taisīn tū hi karī karmēn tyāñcā tō ghēunī dhaḍā ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says seekers of liberation acted with this knowledge before; Arjuna too should act as they did, following their example.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: नेणती जाणते ते हि काय कर्म अकर्म हें । तुज तें सांगतों कर्म जाणूनि सुटशील जें
verses.roman: nēṇatī jāṇatē tē hi kāy karm akarm hēṁ । tuj tēṁ sāṅgatōṅ karm jāṇūni suṭaśīl jēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says even the wise are confused about action and inaction; he will explain action, knowing which Arjuna will be freed.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: सामान्य कर्म जाणावें विकर्म हि विशेष जें । अकर्म तें हि जाणावें कर्माचें तत्व खोल हें
verses.roman: sāmāny karm jāṇāvēṁ vikarm hi viśēṣ jēṁ । akarm tēṁ hi jāṇāvēṅ karmācēn tatv khōl hēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says one must understand ordinary action, forbidden action, and inaction; the truth of action is profound.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: कर्मीं अकर्म जो पाहे अकर्मीं कर्म जो तसें । तो बुध्दिमंत लोकांत तो योगी कृत-कृत्य तो ॥
verses.roman: karmīṁ akarm jō pāhē akarmīṅ karm jō tasēṁ । tō budhdimant lōkānt tō yōgī kr̥ta-kr̥ty tō ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says one who sees inaction in action and action in inaction is wise, a yogi who has fulfilled all his duties.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: उद्योग करितो सारे काम-संकल्प सोडुनी । ज्ञानानें जाळिलीं कर्में म्हणती त्यास पंडित
verses.roman: udyōg karitō sārē kāma-saṅkalp sōḍunī । jñānānēñ jāḷilīṅ karmēm mhaṇatī tyās paṇḍit
verses.summary: Krishna says the one whose every undertaking is free of selfish desire, his deeds burnt by knowledge, is called wise.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: नित्य-तृप्त निराधार न राखे फल-वासना । गेला गढूनि कर्मांत तरी कांहीं करी चि ना ।
verses.roman: nitya-tr̥pt nirādhār n rākhē phala-vāsanā । gēlā gaḍhūni karmānt tarī kāṁhīṅ karī ci nā ।
verses.summary: Krishna says one ever-content, dependent on nothing, with no longing for fruit, does nothing at all even while immersed in action.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: संयमी सोडुनी सर्व इच्छेसह परिग्रह । शरीरें चि करी कर्म दोष त्यास न लागतो
verses.roman: saṁyamī sōḍunī sarv icchēsah parigrah । śarīrēñ ci karī karm dōṣ tyās n lāgatō
verses.summary: Krishna says the self-controlled one, abandoning desire and possessions, acting only with the body, incurs no fault.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: मिळे तेंचि करी गोड न जाणे द्वंद्व मत्सर फळो जळो जया एक करुनि हि न बांधिला ।
verses.roman: miḷē tēñci karī gōḍ n jāṇē dvandv matsar phaḷō jaḷō jayā ēk karuni hi n bāndhilā ।
verses.summary: Krishna says one content with whatever comes, beyond dualities and envy, even-minded in success or failure, is not bound though he acts.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ज्ञानांत बैसली वृत्ती संग सोडूनि मोकळा । यज्ञार्थ करितो कर्म जाय सर्व जिरुनि तें
verses.roman: jñānānt baisalī vr̥ttī saṅg sōḍūni mōkaḷā । yajñārth karitō karm jāy sarv jiruni tēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says for one freed of attachment, mind settled in knowledge, acting as sacrifice, all action dissolves away.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ब्रह्मांत होमिलें ब्रह्म ब्रह्मानें ब्रह्म लक्षुनी । ब्रह्मीं मिसळलें कर्म तेव्हां ब्रह्म चि पावला ॥
verses.roman: brahmānt hōmilēm brahm brahmānēm brahm lakṣunī । brahmīm misaḷalēṅ karm tēvhām brahm ci pāvalā ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says when offering, fire, oblation and offerer are all seen as Brahman, one attains Brahman itself.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: देवताराधनें यज्ञ योगी कोणी अनुष्ठिती । ब्रह्माग्नींत तसे कोणी यज्ञें यज्ञत्व जाळिती
verses.roman: dēvatārādhanēṁ yajñ yōgī kōṇī anuṣṭhitī । brahmāgnīnt tasē kōṇī yajñēṁ yajñatv jāḷitī
verses.summary: Krishna says some yogis perform sacrifice by worshipping gods, while others offer the sacrifice itself into the fire of Brahman.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: श्रोत्रादि इंद्रियें कोणी संयमाग्नींत होमिती । कोणी विषय शब्दादि इंद्रियाग्नींत अर्पिती
verses.roman: śrōtrādi indriyēṅ kōṇī saṁyamāgnīnt hōmitī । kōṇī viṣay śabdādi indriyāgnīnt arpitī
verses.summary: Krishna says some offer their senses like hearing into the fire of restraint; others offer sense-objects like sound into the fire of the senses.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: प्राणेंद्रिय-क्रिया कोणी सर्व होमूनि टाकिती । चिंतनानें समाधीस अंतरीं चेतवूनियां
verses.roman: prāṇēndriya-kriyā kōṇī sarv hōmūni ṭākitī । cintanānēṁ samādhīs antarīñ cētavūniyāṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says some offer all the activities of the senses and vital breath, kindling inner absorption through meditation.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: द्रव्यें जपें तपें योगें चिंतनें वा अशापरी । संयमी यजिती यज्ञ व्रतें प्रखर राखुनी ।
verses.roman: dravyēñ japēn tapēṁ yōgēñ cintanēṁ vā aśāparī । saṁyamī yajitī yajñ vratēm prakhar rākhunī ।
verses.summary: Krishna says some sacrifice through wealth, austerity, yoga, study or meditation, keeping strict vows.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: होमिती एकमेकांत कोणी प्राण-अपान ते । रोधूनि गति दोहींची प्राणायामास साधिती
verses.roman: hōmitī ēkamēkānt kōṇī prāṇa-apān tē । rōdhūni gati dōhīñcī prāṇāyāmās sādhitī
verses.summary: Krishna says some offer the in-breath and out-breath into each other, restraining both to practice breath-control.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: प्राणांत होमिती प्राण कोणी आहार तोडुनी । यज्ञ-वेत्ते चि हे सारे यज्ञाने दोष जाळिती ।
verses.roman: prāṇānt hōmitī prāṇ kōṇī āhār tōḍunī । yajña-vēttē ci hē sārē yajñānē dōṣ jāḷitī ।
verses.summary: Krishna says some restrict food and offer breath into breath; all these know sacrifice and burn their faults through it.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: यज्ञ-शेषामृतें धाले पावले ब्रह्म शाश्वत । न यज्ञाविण हा लोक कोठूनि पर-लोक तो
verses.roman: yajña-śēṣāmr̥tēn dhālē pāvalē brahm śāśvat । n yajñāviṇ hā lōk kōṭhūni para-lōk tō
verses.summary: Krishna says those who taste the nectar-remnant of sacrifice reach eternal Brahman; without sacrifice one has neither this world nor the next.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: विशेष बोलिलें वेदें असे यज्ञ अनेक हे । कर्मानें घडिले जाण जाणूनि सुटशील तूं ॥
verses.roman: viśēṣ bōlilēṁ vēdēṁ asē yajñ anēk hē । karmānēṅ ghaḍilē jāṇ jāṇūni suṭaśīl tūṁ ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says many such sacrifices are described in the Vedas, all born of action; knowing this, Arjuna will be freed.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: द्रव्य यज्ञांहुनी थोर ज्ञान-यज्ञ चि थोर तो । पावती सगळीं कर्में अंतीं ज्ञानांत पूर्णता
verses.roman: dravy yajñāṁhunī thōr jñāna-yajñ ci thōr tō । pāvatī sagaḷīṅ karmēṁ antīñ jñānānt pūrṇatā
verses.summary: Krishna says the sacrifice of knowledge is greater than the sacrifice of material things, for all action culminates fully in knowledge.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: सेवा करुनि तें जाण नम्र-भावें पुसूनियां । ज्ञानोपदेश देतील ज्ञानी अनुभवी तुज ।
verses.roman: sēvā karuni tēñ jāṇ namra-bhāvēm pusūniyāṁ । jñānōpadēś dētīl jñānī anubhavī tuj ।
verses.summary: Krishna says learn this by serving the wise, asking humbly; those who have realized the truth will teach you knowledge.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ज्या ज्ञानानें असा मोह न पुन्हां पावशील तूं । आत्म्यांत आणि माझ्यांत भूतें निःशेष देखुनी
verses.roman: jyā jñānānēṁ asā mōh n punhām pāvaśīl tūṁ । ātmyānt āṇi mājhyānt bhūtēn niḥśēṣ dēkhunī
verses.summary: Krishna says with this knowledge you will not fall into delusion again, seeing all beings in yourself and in him.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: जरी पाप्यांमधें पापी असशील शिरो-मणि । तरी ह्या ज्ञान-नौकेनें पाप तें तरशील तूं
verses.roman: jarī pāpyāmmadhēm pāpī asaśīl śirō-maṇi । tarī hyā jñāna-naukēnēm pāp tēn taraśīl tūṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says even if you are the worst of all sinners, you will cross over all wrongdoing with the boat of knowledge.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: संपूर्ण पेटला अग्नि काष्ठें जाळूनि टाकितो । तसा ज्ञानाग्नि तो सर्व कर्में जाळूनि टाकितो
verses.roman: sampūrṇ pēṭalā agni kāṣṭhēñ jāḷūni ṭākitō । tasā jñānāgni tō sarv karmēñ jāḷūni ṭākitō
verses.summary: Krishna says as a blazing fire reduces wood to ashes, the fire of knowledge burns up all actions.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: यज्ञासम नसे कांहीं पवित्र दुसरें जगीं । योग-युक्त यथा-काळीं तें पावे अंतरीं स्वयें
verses.roman: yajñāsam nasē kāṁhīm pavitr dusarēñ jagīṁ । yōga-yukt yathā-kāḷīn tēm pāvē antarīṁ svayēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says nothing in the world purifies like knowledge; one perfected in yoga finds it within himself in time.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: श्रध्देनें मेळवी ज्ञान संयमी नित्य सावध । ज्ञानानें शीघ्र तो पावे शांति शेवटची मग
verses.roman: śradhdēnēm mēḷavī jñān saṁyamī nity sāvadh । jñānānēṁ śīghr tō pāvē śānti śēvaṭacī mag
verses.summary: Krishna says the person of faith, self-controlled and attentive, gains knowledge and soon attains supreme peace.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: नसे ज्ञान नसे श्रध्दा संशयी नासला पुरा । न हा लोक न तो लोक न पावे सुख संशयी।
verses.roman: nasē jñān nasē śradhdā saṁśayī nāsalā purā । n hā lōk n tō lōk n pāvē sukh saṁśayī।
verses.summary: Krishna says the ignorant, faithless, doubting person is ruined; the doubter has neither this world nor the next nor any happiness.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: योगानें झाडिलीं कर्में ज्ञानें संशय तोडिलें । जो सावधान आत्म्यांत कर्में त्यास न बांधिती
verses.roman: yōgānēñ jhāḍilīṅ karmēñ jñānēṁ saṁśay tōḍilēṁ । jō sāvadhān ātmyānt karmēn tyās n bāndhitī
verses.summary: Krishna says one who has shed action through yoga and cut doubt through knowledge, alert in the self, is not bound by actions.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: म्हणूनि अंतरांतील अज्ञान-कृत संशय । तोडुनी ज्ञान-खडगानें ऊठ तूं योग साधुनी ॥
verses.roman: mhaṇūni antarāntīl ajñāna-kr̥t saṁśay । tōḍunī jñāna-khaḍagānēṁ ūṭh tūṁ yōg sādhunī ॥
verses.summary: Krishna tells Arjuna to cut the doubt born of ignorance in his heart with the sword of knowledge, and rise to practice yoga.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: कृष्णा संन्यास कर्माचा तसा योग हि सांगसी । दोहोंत जें बरें एक सांग तें मज निश्चित ।
verses.roman: kr̥ṣṇā sannyās karmācā tasā yōg hi sāṅgasī । dōhōnt jēm barēṁ ēk sāṅg tēm maj niścit ।
verses.summary: Arjuna asks Krishna to settle which is better—renouncing action altogether or the path of action (yoga)—since Krishna seems to praise both.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: योग संन्यास हे दोन्ही मोक्ष-साधक सारखे । विशेष चि परी योग संन्यासाहूनि मानिला ।
verses.roman: yōg sannyās hē dōnhī mōkṣa-sādhak sārakhē । viśēṣ ci parī yōg sannyāsāhūni mānilā ।
verses.summary: Krishna replies that both renunciation and action lead to liberation, but of the two, the path of action is the better.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: तो जाण नित्य संन्यासी राग-द्वेष नसे जया । जो व्दंव्दावेगळा झाला सुखें बंधांतुनी सुटे
verses.roman: tō jāṇ nity sannyāsī rāga-dvēṣ nasē jayā । jō vdaṁvdāvēgaḷā jhālā sukhēm bandhāntunī suṭē
verses.summary: Krishna says the true renunciate is one who has no likes or dislikes; free of opposites, he is easily released from bondage.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: म्हणती सांख्य-योगांतें भिन्न मूढ न जाणते । बाणो एक हि ती निष्ठा दोहींचें फळ देतसे
verses.roman: mhaṇatī sāṅkhya-yōgāntēm bhinn mūḍh n jāṇatē । bāṇō ēk hi tī niṣṭhā dōhīñcēm phaḷ dētasē
verses.summary: Only the foolish call Sankhya and Yoga different; firmly following either one yields the fruit of both.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: सांख्यांस जें मिळे स्थान तें योग्यांस हि लाभतें । एक-रूप चि दोन्ही जो पाहे तो चि पाहतो ।
verses.roman: sāṅkhyāṁs jēm miḷē sthān tēṁ yōgyāṁs hi lābhatēṁ । ēka-rūp ci dōnhī jō pāhē tō ci pāhatō ।
verses.summary: The state reached by the Sankhya path is also reached by the Yoga path; whoever sees the two as one truly sees.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: योगावांचूनि संन्यास कधीं साधे चि ना सुखें । संयमी योग जोडूनि ब्रह्म शीघ्र चि गांठतो ॥
verses.roman: yōgāvāñcūni sannyās kadhīṁ sādhē ci nā sukhēṁ । saṁyamī yōg jōḍūni brahm śīghr ci gāṇṭhatō ॥
verses.summary: Renunciation is hard to attain without action; the disciplined one who practices yoga quickly reaches Brahman.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अंतरीं धुतला योगी जिंकुनी मन इंद्रियें झाला जीव चि भूतांचा करुनि हि अलिप्त तो
verses.roman: antarīn dhutalā yōgī jiṅkunī man indriyēñ jhālā jīv ci bhūtāñcā karuni hi alipt tō
verses.summary: The yogi purified within, master of mind and senses, identified with all beings, remains untouched even while acting.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: न कांहीं मी करी ऐसें योगी तत्वज्ञ जाणुनी । देखे ऐके शिवे हुंगे खाय जाय निजे श्वसे
verses.roman: n kāṁhīm mī karī aisēṁ yōgī tatvajñ jāṇunī । dēkhē aikē śivē huṅgē khāy jāy nijē śvasē
verses.summary: The wise yogi feels "I do nothing," knowing that seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, moving, sleeping, breathing happen on their own.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: बोले सोडी धरी किंवा पापणी हालवी जरी । इंद्रियें आपुल्या अर्थीं वागती तें चि पाहतो ।
verses.roman: bōlē sōḍī dharī kiṁvā pāpaṇī hālavī jarī । indriyēṁ āpulyā arthīṁ vāgatī tēñ ci pāhatō ।
verses.summary: Whether speaking, releasing, grasping, or blinking, he sees only the senses operating among their objects.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ब्रह्मीं ठेवूनियां कर्में संग सोडूनि जो करी । पापें न लिप्त तो होय पदम-पत्र जसें जळें
verses.roman: brahmīṇ ṭhēvūniyāṅ karmēṁ saṅg sōḍūni jō karī । pāpēn n lipt tō hōy padama-patr jasēñ jaḷēṁ
verses.summary: One who acts offering deeds to Brahman, free of attachment, is untouched by sin like a lotus leaf by water.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: देहा-मनानें बुध्दीनें इंद्रियांनि हि केवळ । आत्म-शुध्दयर्थ निःसंग योगी कर्म अनुष्ठिती
verses.roman: dēhā-manānēm budhdīnēṁ indriyānni hi kēvaḷ । ātma-śudhdayarth niḥsaṅg yōgī karm anuṣṭhitī
verses.summary: Yogis act with body, mind, intellect, and senses, free of attachment, only for self-purification.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: युक्त तो फळ सोडूनि शांति निश्चल पावतो । अयुक्त स्वैर-वृत्तीनें फळीं आसक्त बांधिला ॥
verses.roman: yukt tō phaḷ sōḍūni śānti niścal pāvatō । ayukt svaira-vr̥ttīnēm phaḷīṁ āsakt bāndhilā ॥
verses.summary: The disciplined one gives up the fruit and gains lasting peace; the undisciplined, attached to fruit by desire, stays bound.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: मनानें सगळीं कर्में सोडुनी संयमी सुखें । नव-व्दार-पुरीं राहे करी ना करवी हि ना
verses.roman: manānēṁ sagaḷīṅ karmēṁ sōḍunī saṁyamī sukhēṁ । nava-vdāra-purīṁ rāhē karī nā karavī hi nā
verses.summary: Renouncing all actions mentally, the self-controlled one dwells happily in the body's nine-gated city, neither acting nor causing action.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: न कर्तेपण लोकांचें न कर्में निर्मितो पभू । न कर्मीं फल संयोग स्वभावें सर्व होतसे
verses.roman: n kartēpaṇ lōkāñcēn n karmēn nirmitō pabhū । n karmīm phal saṁyōg svabhāvēṁ sarv hōtasē
verses.summary: Krishna says the Lord creates neither people's agency nor their actions nor the link to results; all happens by nature.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: न घे पाप हि कोणाचें न वा पुण्य हि तो विभु । अज्ञानें झंकिलें ज्ञान त्यामुळें जीव मोहित
verses.roman: n ghē pāp hi kōṇācēn n vā puṇy hi tō vibhu । ajñānēñ jhaṅkilēñ jñān tyāmuḷēñ jīv mōhit
verses.summary: The all-pervading Lord takes on no one's sin or merit; knowledge is veiled by ignorance, which deludes beings.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: गेलें अज्ञान तें ज्यांचें आत्म-ज्ञानें तयां मग । पर-ब्रह्म दिसे स्वच्छ जणूं सूर्यें प्रकाशिलें
verses.roman: gēlēṁ ajñān tēñ jyāñcēṁ ātma-jñānēn tayām mag । para-brahm disē svacch jaṇūṁ sūryēm prakāśilēṁ
verses.summary: For those whose ignorance is destroyed by self-knowledge, the supreme Brahman shines clear like a sun-lit world.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: रंगले त्यांत ओतूनि बुद्धि निश्चय जीवन । पुन्हां येती न माघारें ज्ञानें पाप धुवूनियां ।
verses.roman: raṅgalē tyānt ōtūni buddhi niścay jīvan । punhāṁ yētī n māghārēñ jñānēm pāp dhuvūniyāṁ ।
verses.summary: Those who immerse mind, intellect, and life in Brahman, cleansed of sin by knowledge, never return.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: विद्या-विनय संपन्न द्विज गाय तसा गज । श्वान चांडाळ हे सारे तत्व-ज्ञ सम पाहती
verses.roman: vidyā-vinay sampann dvij gāy tasā gaj । śvān cāṇḍāḷ hē sārē tatva-jñ sam pāhatī
verses.summary: The wise see with equal eye a learned brahmin, a cow, an elephant, a dog, and an outcaste.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: इथें चि जिंकिला जन्म समत्वीं मन रोवुनी । निर्दोष सम जें ब्रह्म झालें तेथें चि तें स्थिर
verses.roman: ithēñ ci jiṅkilā janm samatvīm man rōvunī । nirdōṣ sam jēm brahm jhālēn tēthēñ ci tēṁ sthir
verses.summary: Those who fix the mind in equanimity conquer rebirth here itself; flawless and balanced, they rest in Brahman.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: प्रिय-लाभें नको हर्ष नको उद्वेग अप्रियें । बुद्धि निश्चल निर्मोह ब्रह्मीं ज्ञानी स्थिरावला ।
verses.roman: priya-lābhēn nakō harṣ nakō udvēg apriyēṁ । buddhi niścal nirmōh brahmīñ jñānī sthirāvalā ।
verses.summary: No elation at the pleasant, no distress at the unpleasant—steady, unconfused, the knower is established in Brahman.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: विटला विषयीं जाणे अंतरीं सुख काय तें । ब्रह्मीं मिसळला तेव्हां भोगी तें सुख अक्षय
verses.roman: viṭalā viṣayīñ jāṇē antarīṁ sukh kāy tēṁ । brahmīm misaḷalā tēvhām bhōgī tēṁ sukh akṣay
verses.summary: Detached from external objects, one finds the inner joy; united with Brahman, he enjoys imperishable happiness.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: विषयांतील जे भोग ते दुःखास चि कारण । येती जसे तसे जाती विवेकी न रमे तिथें
verses.roman: viṣayāntīl jē bhōg tē duḥkhās ci kāraṇ । yētī jasē tasē jātī vivēkī n ramē tithēṁ
