verses.summary: Sense pleasures are sources of pain; they come and go, so the wise person does not delight in them.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: प्रयत्नें मरणापूर्वीं ह्या देहीं जिरवूं शके । काम-क्रोधांतले वेग तो योगी तो खरा सुखी ।
verses.roman: prayatnēm maraṇāpūrvīṁ hyā dēhīñ jiravūṁ śakē । kāma-krōdhāntalē vēg tō yōgī tō kharā sukhī ।
verses.summary: One who can withstand the impulses of desire and anger before death is the true yogi, truly happy.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: प्रकाश स्थिरता सौख्य अंतरीं लाभलीं ज्या । ब्रह्म होऊनि तो योगी ब्रह्म-निर्वाण मेळवी
verses.roman: prakāś sthiratā saukhy antarīṁ lābhalīñ jyā । brahm hōūni tō yōgī brahma-nirvāṇ mēḷavī
verses.summary: The yogi whose light, steadiness, and joy are within becomes Brahman and attains liberation in Brahman.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: फिटले दोष शंका हि मुठींत धरिलें मन । पावले ब्रह्म-निर्वाण ज्ञानी विश्व हितीं रत
verses.roman: phiṭalē dōṣ śaṅkā hi muṭhīnt dharilēm man । pāvalē brahma-nirvāṇ jñānī viśv hitīṁ rat
verses.summary: Those whose flaws and doubts are gone, mind controlled, devoted to all beings' welfare, attain liberation in Brahman.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: काम-क्रोधांस जिंकूनि यत्नें चित्तास बांधिती । देखिती ब्रह्म-निर्वाण आत्म-ज्ञानी चहूंकडे ॥
verses.roman: kāma-krōdhāṁs jiṅkūni yatnēñ cittās bāndhitī । dēkhitī brahma-nirvāṇ ātma-jñānī cahūṅkaḍē ॥
verses.summary: Conquering desire and anger, controlling the mind, the self-knowers find liberation in Brahman all around them.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: विषयांचा बहिष्कार डोळा भ्रू-संग्रमीं स्थिर । करुनि नासिका-स्थानीं प्राणापान हि सारखें
verses.roman: viṣayāñcā bahiṣkār ḍōḷā bhrū-saṅgramīṁ sthir । karuni nāsikā-sthānīm prāṇāpān hi sārakhēṁ
verses.summary: Shutting out external objects, fixing the gaze between the brows, balancing the breath in the nostrils—

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: आवरी मुनी मोक्षार्थी इंद्रियें मन बुध्दि जो । सोडी इच्छा भय-क्रोध सर्वदा सुटला चि तो
verses.roman: āvarī munī mōkṣārthī indriyēm man budhdi jō । sōḍī icchā bhaya-krōdh sarvadā suṭalā ci tō
verses.summary: The sage seeking liberation who masters senses, mind, and intellect, and casts off desire, fear, and anger, is ever free.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: भोक्ता यज्ञ-तपांचा मो सोयरा विश्व-चालक । जाणूनि ह्यापरी मातें शांतीस वरिला चि तो ॥
verses.roman: bhōktā yajña-tapāñcā mō sōyarā viśva-cālak । jāṇūni hyāparī mātēṁ śāntīs varilā ci tō ॥
verses.summary: Knowing me as the enjoyer of sacrifices and austerities, friend of all, ruler of the world—such a one attains peace.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: फळी आश्रय सोडूनि करी कर्तव्य कर्म जो । तो संन्यासी तसा योगी न जो निर्यज्ञ निष्क्रिय
verses.roman: phaḷī āśray sōḍūni karī kartavy karm jō । tō sannyāsī tasā yōgī n jō niryajñ niṣkriy
verses.summary: Krishna says the true renouncer and yogi is one who does his duty without depending on the fruit, not one who merely drops rituals and action.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: संन्यास म्हणती ज्यास योग तो जाण पांडवा । सोडल्यावीण संकल्प कोणी योगी न होतसे ।
verses.roman: sannyās mhaṇatī jyās yōg tō jāṇ pāṇḍavā । sōḍalyāvīṇ saṅkalp kōṇī yōgī n hōtasē ।
verses.summary: Krishna tells Arjuna that what is called renunciation is itself yoga; no one becomes a yogi without giving up self-centered intentions.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: योगावरी चढूं जातां कर्म साधन बोलिलें । योगी आरूढ तो होतां शम साधन बोलिलें
verses.roman: yōgāvarī caḍhūñ jātāṅ karm sādhan bōlilēṁ । yōgī ārūḍh tō hōtāṁ śam sādhan bōlilēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says action is the means for one climbing toward yoga, while calmness is the means for one already established in it.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: कर्मांत जो अनासक्त विरक्त विषयीं असे । संकल्प सुटले तेव्हां तो योगारूढ बेलिला ॥
verses.roman: karmānt jō anāsakt virakt viṣayīṁ asē । saṅkalp suṭalē tēvhān tō yōgārūḍh bēlilā ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says when a person is unattached to action and sense objects and free of intentions, he is called established in yoga.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: उद्धरावा स्वयें आत्मा खचूं देऊं नये कधीं । आत्मा चि आपुला बंधु आत्मा चि रिपु आपुला
verses.roman: uddharāvā svayēṁ ātmā khacūn dēūn nayē kadhīṁ । ātmā ci āpulā bandhu ātmā ci ripu āpulā
verses.summary: Krishna says one should lift oneself up and never let oneself sink; the self alone is one's own friend and one's own enemy.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: जिंकूनि घेतला आत्मा बंधु तो होय आपुला । सोडिला तो जरी स्वैर शत्रुत्व करितो स्वयें
verses.roman: jiṅkūni ghētalā ātmā bandhu tō hōy āpulā । sōḍilā tō jarī svair śatrutv karitō svayēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says the self conquered becomes one's friend, but left uncontrolled it acts as one's own enemy.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: जितात्मा शांत जो झाला देखे ब्रह्म चि एकलें । मानापमानीं शीतोष्णीं सुख-दुःखीं समावलें
verses.roman: jitātmā śānt jō jhālā dēkhē brahm ci ēkalēṁ । mānāpamānīṁ śītōṣṇīṁ sukha-duḥkhīṁ samāvalēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says the self-conquered, peaceful person sees only Brahman, remaining balanced in honor and dishonor, heat and cold, pleasure and pain.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: तोषला ज्ञान-विज्ञानें स्थिर जिंकूनि इंद्रियें । तो योगी सम जो देखे सोनें पाषाण मृत्तिका
verses.roman: tōṣalā jñāna-vijñānēṁ sthir jiṅkūni indriyēṁ । tō yōgī sam jō dēkhē sōnēm pāṣāṇ mr̥ttikā
verses.summary: Krishna says the yogi content in knowledge and realization, steady with senses controlled, sees gold, stone, and clay as the same.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: शत्रु मित्र उदासीन मध्यस्थ परका सखा । असो साधु असो पापी सम पाहे विशेष तो ॥
verses.roman: śatru mitr udāsīn madhyasth parakā sakhā । asō sādhu asō pāpī sam pāhē viśēṣ tō ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says one who views friend, foe, neutral, stranger, kin, saint, and sinner all alike is distinguished.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: साधकें चित्त बांधूनि इच्छा संग्रह सोडुनी । आत्म्यास नित्य जोडावें एकांतीं एकलेपणे
verses.roman: sādhakēñ citt bāndhūni icchā saṅgrah sōḍunī । ātmyās nity jōḍāvēṁ ēkāntīṁ ēkalēpaṇē
verses.summary: Krishna says the seeker should constantly unite with the self, alone in solitude, controlling the mind and giving up desires and possessions.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: पवित्र स्थान पाहूनि घालावें स्थिर आसन । दर्भ चर्म वरी वस्त्र न घ्यावें उंच नीच तें
verses.roman: pavitr sthān pāhūni ghālāvēṁ sthir āsan । darbh carm varī vastr n ghyāvēṁ uñc nīc tēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says to find a clean spot and set up a firm seat of kusha grass, skin, and cloth, neither too high nor too low.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: चित्तेंद्रियांचे व्यापार वारावे तेथ बैसुनी । आत्म शुद्ध्यर्थ जोडावा योग एकाग्र मानसें
verses.roman: cittēndriyāñcē vyāpār vārāvē tēth baisunī । ātm śuddhyarth jōḍāvā yōg ēkāgr mānasēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says, sitting there, one should restrain the activity of mind and senses and practice yoga with a one-pointed mind for purifying the self.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: शरीर सम-रेखेंत राखावें स्थिर निश्चळ । दृष्टी ठेवूनि नासाग्रीं न पहावें कुणीकडे
verses.roman: śarīr sama-rēkhēnt rākhāvēṁ sthir niścaḷ । dr̥ṣṭī ṭhēvūni nāsāgrīn n pahāvēṅ kuṇīkaḍē
verses.summary: Krishna says keep the body straight, steady, and still, gaze fixed at the tip of the nose, not looking around.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: शांति निर्भय मच्चित्त ब्रह्मचर्य-व्रती स्थिर । मन रोधूनि युक्तीनें रहावें मत्परायण
verses.roman: śānti nirbhay maccitt brahmacarya-vratī sthir । man rōdhūni yuktīnēṁ rahāvēm matparāyaṇ
verses.summary: Krishna says the calm, fearless one, firm in celibacy, should restrain the mind and sit devoted to Krishna with thoughts fixed on him.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: असें आत्म्यास जोडूनि योगी आवरिला मनें । मोक्षास भिडली शांति माझिया ठाईं चि मेळवी
verses.roman: asēṁ ātmyās jōḍūni yōgī āvarilā manēṁ । mōkṣās bhiḍalī śānti mājhiyā ṭhāīñ ci mēḷavī
verses.summary: Krishna says the yogi who thus unites the self with controlled mind attains the peace bordering on liberation, resting in Krishna.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: न योग फार खाऊनि किंवां खाणें चि सोडुनी । न फार झोंप घेऊनि किंवां जागत बैसुनी
verses.roman: n yōg phār khāūni kiṁvāṅ khāṇēñ ci sōḍunī । n phār jhōmp ghēūni kiṁvāñ jāgat baisunī
verses.summary: Krishna says yoga is not for one who eats too much or not at all, nor for one who sleeps too much or stays awake.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: निजणें जागणें खाणें फिरणें आणि कार्य हि । मोजूनि करितो त्यास योग हा दुःख नाशन ॥
verses.roman: nijaṇēñ jāgaṇēṅ khāṇēm phiraṇēṁ āṇi kāry hi । mōjūni karitō tyās yōg hā duḥkh nāśan ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says for one moderate in sleep, waking, eating, activity, and work, this yoga destroys suffering.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: संपूर्ण नेमिलें चित्त आत्म-रूपीं चि रंगला । निमाली वासना तेव्हां योगी तो युक्त बोलिला
verses.roman: sampūrṇ nēmilēñ citt ātma-rūpīñ ci raṅgalā । nimālī vāsanā tēvhāṁ yōgī tō yukt bōlilā
verses.summary: Krishna says when the disciplined mind rests in the self alone and desire is gone, the yogi is said to be united.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: निर्वातीं ठेविला दीप तेवतो एकसारखा । तसें आत्मानुसंधानि योग्याचें चित्त वर्णिती ।
verses.roman: nirvātīṇ ṭhēvilā dīp tēvatō ēkasārakhā । tasēṁ ātmānusandhāni yōgyācēñ citt varṇitī ।
verses.summary: Krishna says a lamp kept where there is no wind burns steady; such is the controlled mind of the yogi absorbed in the self.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: निरोधें जेथ चित्ताचा सर्व संचार संपला । जेथ भेटूनि आत्म्यातें अंतरीं तोषला स्वयें
verses.roman: nirōdhēñ jēth cittācā sarv sañcār sampalā । jēth bhēṭūni ātmyātēṁ antarīn tōṣalā svayēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna describes the state where mental activity ceases through restraint, and one is satisfied within meeting the self.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: भोगूनि इंद्रियातीत बुद्धि-गम्य महा-सुख । न ढळे चि कधीं जेथ तत्वापासूनि लेश हि
verses.roman: bhōgūni indriyātīt buddhi-gamy mahā-sukh । n ḍhaḷē ci kadhīñ jēth tatvāpāsūni lēś hi
verses.summary: Krishna says there one enjoys the boundless joy beyond the senses, grasped by the intellect, and never strays from that truth.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: जया लाभापुढें लाभ दुसरा तुच्छ लेखितो । न चळे जेथ राहूनि दुःख-भारें हि दाटला
verses.roman: jayā lābhāpuḍhēṁ lābh dusarā tucch lēkhitō । n caḷē jēth rāhūni duḥkha-bhārēṁ hi dāṭalā
verses.summary: Krishna says, having gained which one counts no other gain greater, and is unshaken even by the weight of heavy sorrow.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: तयास म्हणती योग दुःखाचा जो वियोग चि जोडावा निश्चयानें तो योग उत्साह राखुनी ॥
verses.roman: tayās mhaṇatī yōg duḥkhācā jō viyōg ci jōḍāvā niścayānēn tō yōg utsāh rākhunī ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says this severance from suffering is called yoga, to be practiced with determination and enthusiasm.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: संकल्पीं उठिले सारे काम निःशेष सोडुनी । इंद्रियें ही मनाने चि ओढूनि विषयांतुनी
verses.roman: saṅkalpīṁ uṭhilē sārē kām niḥśēṣ sōḍunī । indriyēṁ hī manānē ci ōḍhūni viṣayāntunī
verses.summary: Krishna says abandon completely all desires born of intention, and draw the senses back from their objects with the mind.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: धरुनि धीर बुद्धीनें निवर्तावें हळूं हळूं । आत्म्यांत मन रोवूनि कांहीं चिंतूं नय स्वयें
verses.roman: dharuni dhīr buddhīnēn nivartāvēṁ haḷūṁ haḷūṁ । ātmyānt man rōvūni kāṁhīñ cintūn nay svayēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says with a steady intellect withdraw little by little, fix the mind on the self, and think of nothing else.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: फुटेल जेथजेथूनि मन चंचल अस्थिर । तेथतेथूनि बांधूनि लावावें आत्म-चिंतनीं ।
verses.roman: phuṭēl jēthajēthūni man cañcal asthir । tēthatēthūni bāndhūni lāvāvēṁ ātma-cintanīṁ ।
verses.summary: Krishna says wherever the restless, unsteady mind wanders off, draw it back and fix it on contemplation of the self.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: विकारांसह तें ज्याचें शमलें मन निर्मळ । झाला ब्रह्म चि तो योगी पावला सुख उत्तम
verses.roman: vikārāṁsah tēñ jyācēṁ śamalēm man nirmaḷ । jhālā brahm ci tō yōgī pāvalā sukh uttam
verses.summary: Krishna says the yogi whose mind is calmed and purified, free of disturbances, becomes Brahman and attains the highest joy.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: आत्म्यास नित्य जोडूनि ह्यापरी दोष जाळुनी । सुखें चि भोगितो योगी ब्रह्मानंद अपार तो ॥
verses.roman: ātmyās nity jōḍūni hyāparī dōṣ jāḷunī । sukhēñ ci bhōgitō yōgī brahmānand apār tō ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says by constantly uniting with the self and thus burning away faults, the yogi easily enjoys boundless bliss of Brahman.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: भूतांत भरला आत्मा भूतें आत्म्यांत राहिलीं । योगानें जोडिला देखे हें चि सर्वत्र दर्शन
verses.roman: bhūtānt bharalā ātmā bhūtēṁ ātmyānt rāhilīṁ । yōgānēñ jōḍilā dēkhē hēñ ci sarvatr darśan
verses.summary: Krishna says the yogi united in yoga sees the self in all beings and all beings in the self, this same vision everywhere.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: मज सर्वांत जो पाहे पाहे माझ्यांत सर्व हि । त्याचा मी आणि तो माझा एकमेकांस अक्षय
verses.roman: maj sarvānt jō pāhē pāhē mājhyānt sarv hi । tyācā mī āṇi tō mājhā ēkamēkāṁs akṣay
verses.summary: Krishna says one who sees him in all and all in him is never lost to him, nor is he lost to that person.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: स्थिर होऊनि एकत्वीं सर्व-भूतीं भजे मज । राहो कसा हि तो योगी माझ्यामध्ये चि राहतो
verses.roman: sthir hōūni ēkatvīṁ sarva-bhūtīm bhajē maj । rāhō kasā hi tō yōgī mājhyāmadhyē ci rāhatō
verses.summary: Krishna says the yogi steady in oneness, worshiping him in all beings, dwells in him whatever his way of living.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: जो आत्मौपम्य बुद्धीनें सर्वत्र सम पाहतो । जसें सुख तसें दुःख तो योगी थोर मानिला ॥
verses.roman: jō ātmaupamy buddhīnēṁ sarvatr sam pāhatō । jasēṁ sukh tasēn duḥkh tō yōgī thōr mānilā ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says the yogi who, by analogy with himself, sees all alike in pleasure and pain is regarded as the highest.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: तूं बोलिलास जो आतां साम्य योग जनार्दना । न देखें स्थिरता त्याची ह्या चंचळ मनामुळें
verses.roman: tūm bōlilās jō ātāṁ sāmy yōg janārdanā । n dēkhēṁ sthiratā tyācī hyā cañcaḷ manāmuḷēṁ
verses.summary: Arjuna says he cannot see steadiness in this yoga of equanimity Krishna described, because the mind is so restless.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: मन चंचळ हें कृष्णा हट्टी छळितसे बळें । धांवे वार्‍यावरी त्याचा दिसे निग्रह दुष्कर
verses.roman: man cañcaḷ hēṅ kr̥ṣṇā haṭṭī chaḷitasē baḷēṁ । dhāṁvē vāryāvarī tyācā disē nigrah duṣkar
verses.summary: Arjuna says the mind is restless, stubborn, and forceful, rushing like the wind, and controlling it seems very hard.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अवश्य मन दुःसाध्य म्हणतोस तसें चि तें । परी अभ्यास-वैराग्यें त्याचा निग्रह होतसे ॥
verses.roman: avaśy man duḥsādhy mhaṇatōs tasēñ ci tēṁ । parī abhyāsa-vairāgyēn tyācā nigrah hōtasē ॥
verses.summary: Krishna agrees the mind is hard to master, but says it can be restrained through practice and detachment.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: श्रद्धा आहे नव्हे यत्न योगांतूनि चळूनि जो । मुकला योग-सिद्धीस जाय कोण्या गतीस ता
verses.roman: śraddhā āhē navhē yatn yōgāntūni caḷūni jō । mukalā yōga-siddhīs jāy kōṇyā gatīs tā
verses.summary: Arjuna asks about one who has faith but not effort, who slips from yoga and misses its perfection—what fate does he reach?

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: काय तो उभय-भ्रष्ट ब्रह्म-मार्गीं भुलूनियां नाश पावे निराधार फुटलेल्या ढगापरी
verses.roman: kāy tō ubhaya-bhraṣṭ brahma-mārgīm bhulūniyān nāś pāvē nirādhār phuṭalēlyā ḍhagāparī
verses.summary: Arjuna asks whether such a person, fallen from both paths and deluded on the path to Brahman, perishes like a scattered cloud.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: माझा संशय हा कृष्णा तूं चि फेडी मुळांतुनी । फेडीलसा दुजा कोणी न दिसे चि तुझ्याविण
verses.roman: mājhā saṁśay hā kr̥ṣṇā tūñ ci phēḍī muḷāntunī । phēḍīlasā dujā kōṇī n disē ci tujhyāviṇ
verses.summary: Arjuna asks Krishna to clear this doubt completely, saying no one else can resolve it.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: न ह्या लोकीं न त्या लोकीं नाश तो पावतो कधीं । शुभकारी कुणी बापा दुर्गतीस न जातसे
verses.roman: n hyā lōkīn n tyā lōkīn nāś tō pāvatō kadhīṁ । śubhakārī kuṇī bāpā durgatīs n jātasē
verses.summary: Krishna says such a person is never lost in this world or the next, for one who does good never comes to a bad end.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: पुण्य-लोकांत राहूनि तो योग-भ्रष्ट संतत । शुचि साधनवंतांच्या घरीं जन्मास येतसे
verses.roman: puṇya-lōkānt rāhūni tō yōga-bhraṣṭ santat । śuci sādhanavantāñcyā gharīñ janmās yētasē
verses.summary: Krishna says the one fallen from yoga dwells in worlds of the righteous, then is reborn in the home of pure, practicing people.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अथवा प्राज्ञ योग्यांच्या कुळीं चि मग जन्मतो । अवश्य हा असा जन्म लोकीं अत्यंत दुर्लभ ।
verses.roman: athavā prājñ yōgyāñcyā kuḷīñ ci mag janmatō । avaśy hā asā janm lōkīṁ atyant durlabh ।
verses.summary: Krishna says, or else he is born into a family of wise yogis; such a birth is very rare in this world.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: तिथें तो पूर्व-जन्मींचा बुद्धि-संस्कार जोडुनी । मोक्षार्थ करितो यत्न पूर्वींहूनि पुढें पुन्हां
verses.roman: tithēn tō pūrva-janmīñcā buddhi-saṁskār jōḍunī । mōkṣārth karitō yatn pūrvīṁhūni puḍhēm punhāṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says there he regains the mental impressions of his former life and strives again for liberation, beyond his earlier effort.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: पूर्वाभ्यास-बळानें तो खेंचला पर-तंत्र चि । जिज्ञासेनेंहि योगाच्या जातो वेदांस लंघुनी
verses.roman: pūrvābhyāsa-baḷānēn tō khēñcalā para-tantr ci । jijñāsēnēṁhi yōgācyā jātō vēdāṁs laṅghunī
verses.summary: Krishna says drawn helplessly by the force of past practice, his very wish for yoga carries him beyond the Vedic rituals.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: योगी तत्पर राहुनी दोष जाळित जाळित । अनेक जन्मीं संपूर्ण होऊनी मोक्ष पावतो ॥
verses.roman: yōgī tatpar rāhunī dōṣ jāḷit jāḷit । anēk janmīṁ sampūrṇ hōūnī mōkṣ pāvatō ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says the striving yogi, burning away faults over many births, becomes perfected and attains liberation.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ज्ञानी तापस कर्मिष्ठ ह्या सर्वांहुनी आगळा । मानिला तो असे योगी योगी होईं म्हणूनि तूं
verses.roman: jñānī tāpas karmiṣṭh hyā sarvāṁhunī āgaḷā । mānilā tō asē yōgī yōgī hōīm mhaṇūni tūṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says the yogi is greater than the ascetic, the learned, and the ritualist; therefore, Arjuna, become a yogi.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: सर्व योग्यांमधे योगी जीव माझ्यांत ठेवुनी । श्रद्धेनें भजतो मातें तो थोर मज वाटतो ॥
verses.roman: sarv yōgyāmmadhē yōgī jīv mājhyānt ṭhēvunī । śraddhēnēm bhajatō mātēn tō thōr maj vāṭatō ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says among all yogis, the one who worships him with faith, his inner self merged in Krishna, is dearest to him.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: प्रीतीनें आसरा माझा घेउनी योग साधित । जाणशील कसें ऐक समग्र मज निश्चित
verses.roman: prītīnēṁ āsarā mājhā ghēunī yōg sādhit । jāṇaśīl kasēṁ aik samagr maj niścit
verses.summary: Krishna tells Arjuna to listen: by taking refuge in him and practicing yoga, Arjuna will come to know him fully and certainly.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: विज्ञानासह तें ज्ञान संपूर्ण तुज सांगतों । तें जाणूनि पुढें येथें जाणावेसें न राहतें
verses.roman: vijñānāsah tēñ jñān sampūrṇ tuj sāṅgatōṁ । tēñ jāṇūni puḍhēṁ yēthēñ jāṇāvēsēn n rāhatēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says he will explain knowledge along with its application completely, after which nothing more remains to be known.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: लक्षावधींत एखादा मोक्षार्थ झटतो कधीं । झटणाऱ्यांत एखादा तत्वतां जाणतो मज
verses.roman: lakṣāvadhīnt ēkhādā mōkṣārth jhaṭatō kadhīṁ । jhaṭaṇāऱ्yānt ēkhādā tatvatāñ jāṇatō maj
verses.summary: Among thousands, rarely one strives for liberation; among those who strive, rarely one truly knows Krishna.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: पृथ्वी आप तसें तेज वायु आकाश पांचवें । मन बुद्धि अहंकार अशी प्रकृति अष्ट धा
verses.roman: pr̥thvī āp tasēn tēj vāyu ākāś pāñcavēṁ । man buddhi ahaṅkār aśī prakr̥ti aṣṭ dhā
verses.summary: Krishna lists his eightfold lower nature: earth, water, fire, air, space, mind, intellect, and ego.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ही झाली अपरा माझी दुसरी जाण ती परा । जीव रुपें जिनें सारें जग हें धरिलें असे
verses.roman: hī jhālī aparā mājhī dusarī jāṇ tī parā । jīv rupēñ jinēṁ sārēñ jag hēn dharilēṁ asē
verses.summary: That is his lower nature; his higher nature is the life-force that sustains this entire world.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ह्या दोन्हीपासुनी भूतें सगळीं जाण निर्मिलीं । साऱ्या जगास तद्-व्दारा मूळ मीं आणि शेवट
verses.roman: hyā dōnhīpāsunī bhūtēṁ sagaḷīñ jāṇ nirmilīṁ । sāऱ्yā jagās tad-vdārā mūḷ mīṁ āṇi śēvaṭ
verses.summary: All beings arise from these two natures; Krishna is the origin and the end of the whole world.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: दुसरें तत्व नाहीं चि कांहीं माझ्या पलीकडे । ओविलें सर्व माझ्यांत जसे धाग्यामधे मणी
verses.roman: dusarēn tatv nāhīñ ci kāṁhīm mājhyā palīkaḍē । ōvilēṁ sarv mājhyānt jasē dhāgyāmadhē maṇī
verses.summary: Nothing exists beyond Krishna; everything is strung on him like beads on a thread.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: पाण्यांत रस मी झालों चंद्र सूर्यीं प्रकाश मी । ॐ वेदीं शब्द आकाशीं पुरुषीं पुरुषार्थ मी
verses.roman: pāṇyānt ras mī jhālōñ candr sūryīm prakāś mī । ॐ vēdīṁ śabd ākāśīm puruṣīm puruṣārth mī
verses.summary: Krishna is the taste in water, light in sun and moon, Om in the Vedas, sound in space, and manliness in men.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: मी पुण्य-गंध पृथ्वींत असे अग्नींत उष्णता । प्राणि-मात्रांत आयुष्य तपो-वृद्धांत मी तप
verses.roman: mī puṇya-gandh pr̥thvīnt asē agnīnt uṣṇatā । prāṇi-mātrānt āyuṣy tapō-vr̥ddhānt mī tap
verses.summary: Krishna is the pure fragrance in earth, heat in fire, life in all beings, and the austerity of ascetics.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: सर्व भूतांत जें बीज तें मी जाण सनातन । बुद्धिमंतांत मी बुद्धि तेजस्व्यांत हि तेज मी
verses.roman: sarv bhūtānt jēm bīj tēm mī jāṇ sanātan । buddhimantānt mī buddhi tējasvyānt hi tēj mī
verses.summary: Krishna is the eternal seed in all beings, the intelligence of the intelligent, and the brilliance of the brilliant.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: वैराग्य-युक्त निष्काम बळवंतांत मी बळ । राहे धरुनि धर्मास ती मी भूतांत वासना
verses.roman: vairāgya-yukt niṣkām baḷavantānt mī baḷ । rāhē dharuni dharmās tī mī bhūtānt vāsanā
verses.summary: Krishna is the desireless strength of the strong, and the desire in beings that stays within righteousness.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: माझ्यांतूनि तिन्ही झाले सात्विकादिक भाव ते । परी त्यांत न मी राहे ते चि माझ्यांत राहती
verses.roman: mājhyāntūni tinhī jhālē sātvikādik bhāv tē । parī tyānt n mī rāhē tē ci mājhyānt rāhatī
verses.summary: All states of being—sattvic and the rest—arise from Krishna; he is not in them, but they are in him.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ह्या गुणात्मक भावांनीं विश्व मोहूनि टाकिलें । ज्यामुळें मी न जाणूं यें गुणातीत सनातन
verses.roman: hyā guṇātmak bhāvānnīṁ viśv mōhūni ṭākilēṁ । jyāmuḷēm mī n jāṇūṁ yēṅ guṇātīt sanātan
verses.summary: Deluded by these three qualities, the world fails to recognize Krishna, who is beyond the qualities and eternal.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: माझी ही त्रिगुणी दैवी माया न तरवे कुणा । कासेस लागले माझ्या ते चि जाती तरुनियां
verses.roman: mājhī hī triguṇī daivī māyā n taravē kuṇā । kāsēs lāgalē mājhyā tē ci jātī taruniyāṁ
verses.summary: His divine threefold maya is hard to cross; only those who take refuge in him cross over it.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: हीन मूढ दुराचारी माझा आश्रय सोडिती मायेनें भ्रांत होऊनि आसुरी भाव जोडिती ।
verses.roman: hīn mūḍh durācārī mājhā āśray sōḍitī māyēnēm bhrānt hōūni āsurī bhāv jōḍitī ।
verses.summary: Evil-doers, fools, and the base do not seek Krishna's refuge; deluded by maya, they take to demonic ways.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: भक्त चौघे सदाचारी भजती मज अर्जुना । ज्ञानी त चि जिज्ञासू हितार्थी आणि विह्वल
verses.roman: bhakt caughē sadācārī bhajatī maj arjunā । jñānī t ci jijñāsū hitārthī āṇi vihval
verses.summary: Four kinds of good people worship Krishna: the distressed, the seeker of knowledge, the seeker of gain, and the wise.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ज्ञानी वरिष्ठ सर्वांत नित्य-युक्त अनन्य जो । अत्यंत गोड मी त्यास तो हि गोड तसा मज
verses.roman: jñānī variṣṭh sarvānt nitya-yukt anany jō । atyant gōḍ mī tyās tō hi gōḍ tasā maj
verses.summary: The wise one, ever steadfast and single-minded, is supreme; Krishna is exceedingly dear to him and he to Krishna.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: उदार हे जरी सारे ज्ञानी तो मी चि कीं स्वयें । जोडला स्थिर माझ्यांत गति अंतिम पाहुनी
verses.roman: udār hē jarī sārē jñānī tō mī ci kīṁ svayēṁ । jōḍalā sthir mājhyānt gati antim pāhunī
verses.summary: Though all are noble, the wise one is Krishna's very self, steadfastly fixed in him, seeking the ultimate goal.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अनेक जन्म घेउनि पावला शरणागति । विश्व देखे वासदेव संत तो बहु-दुर्लभ ॥
verses.roman: anēk janm ghēuni pāvalā śaraṇāgati । viśv dēkhē vāsadēv sant tō bahu-durlabh ॥
verses.summary: After many births the wise one attains surrender, seeing Krishna as everything; such a saint is very rare.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: भ्रमले कामना-ग्रस्त धुंडिती अन्य दैवतें । स्वभाव-वश होऊनि तो तो नियम पाळिती
verses.roman: bhramalē kāmanā-grast dhuṇḍitī any daivatēṁ । svabhāva-vaś hōūni tō tō niyam pāḷitī
verses.summary: Those whose desires distort judgment seek other deities, following various rules according to their own nature.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: श्रद्धेनें ज्या स्वरुपास जे भजूं इच्छिती जसे । त्यांची ती चि तशी श्रद्धा स्थिर मी करितों स्वयें
verses.roman: śraddhēnēñ jyā svarupās jē bhajūṁ icchitī jasē । tyāñcī tī ci taśī śraddhā sthir mī karitōṁ svayēṁ
verses.summary: Whatever form a devotee wishes to worship with faith, Krishna himself makes that faith firm.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: त्या श्रद्धेच्या बळानें ते त्या स्वरुपास पूजिती । मग मागितले भाग पावती मीं चि निर्मिले
verses.roman: tyā śraddhēcyā baḷānēn tē tyā svarupās pūjitī । mag māgitalē bhāg pāvatī mīñ ci nirmilē
verses.summary: With that faith they worship that form and obtain their desired benefits, which are granted by Krishna himself.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अल्प बुद्धीमुळें त्यांस मिळे फळ अशाश्वत । देवांचे भक्त देवांस माझे ते मज पावती
verses.roman: alp buddhīmuḷēn tyāṁs miḷē phaḷ aśāśvat । dēvāñcē bhakt dēvāṁs mājhē tē maj pāvatī
verses.summary: Such limited-minded people get perishable fruit; worshippers of gods reach the gods, but Krishna's devotees reach him.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: व्यक्त मी हें चि ते घेती बुद्धि-हीन जाणुनी । अव्यक्त थोर जें रुप माझें अंतिम शाश्वत
verses.roman: vyakt mī hēñ ci tē ghētī buddhi-hīn jāṇunī । avyakt thōr jēṁ rup mājhēṁ antim śāśvat
verses.summary: The foolish think Krishna's manifest form is all there is, not knowing his higher, unmanifest, eternal nature.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: वेढिलों योग-मायेनें अंधार चि जगास मी । अजन्मा नित्य मी कैसा मूढ कोणी न ओळखे
verses.roman: vēḍhilōṁ yōga-māyēnēṁ andhār ci jagās mī । ajanmā nity mī kaisā mūḍh kōṇī n ōḷakhē
verses.summary: Veiled by his yoga-maya, Krishna is not visible to all; the deluded fail to recognize him as unborn and eternal.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: झालीं जीं जीं हि होतील भूतें आहेत आज जीं । सगळीं जाणतों तीं मी मज कोणी न जाणती ॥
verses.roman: jhālīñ jīñ jīṁ hi hōtīl bhūtēṁ āhēt āj jīṁ । sagaḷīñ jāṇatōn tīm mī maj kōṇī n jāṇatī ॥
verses.summary: Krishna knows all beings of past, present, and future, but no one knows him.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: राग-द्वेषांमुळे चित्तीं जडला व्दंद्व मोह जो । संसारीं सगळीं भूतें त्यानें मोहूनि टाकिलीं
verses.roman: rāga-dvēṣāmmuḷē cittīñ jaḍalā vdandv mōh jō । saṁsārīṁ sagaḷīm bhūtēn tyānēm mōhūni ṭākilīṁ
verses.summary: All beings in the world are deluded by the pairs of opposites arising from attraction and aversion.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ज्यानीं झिजविलें पाप पुण्य कर्में करुनियां । ते व्दंद्व-मोह तोडूनि भजती मज निश्चयें ।
verses.roman: jyānīñ jhijavilēm pāp puṇy karmēṅ karuniyāṁ । tē vdandva-mōh tōḍūni bhajatī maj niścayēṁ ।
verses.summary: Those whose sins have worn away through good deeds break free of this delusion and worship Krishna firmly.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: झटती आश्रयें माझ्या जरा-मृत्यु गिळावया । ते ब्रह्म जाणती पूर्ण तसें अध्यात्म कर्म हि
verses.roman: jhaṭatī āśrayēm mājhyā jarā-mr̥tyu giḷāvayā । tē brahm jāṇatī pūrṇ tasēṁ adhyātm karm hi
verses.summary: Those who take refuge in Krishna to conquer aging and death come to know Brahman, the self, and action fully.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अधिभूताधिदैवांत अधियज्ञांत जे मज । देखती ते प्रयाणीं हि जाणती मज सावध ॥
verses.roman: adhibhūtādhidaivānt adhiyajñānt jē maj । dēkhatī tē prayāṇīṁ hi jāṇatī maj sāvadh ॥
verses.summary: Those who know Krishna as the principle behind beings, gods, and sacrifice keep him in mind even at the time of death.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: ब्रह्म तें बोलिलें काय काय अध्यात्म कर्म तें । अधि-भूत कसें सांग अधि-दैव हि तें तसें
verses.roman: brahm tēm bōlilēṅ kāy kāy adhyātm karm tēṁ । adhi-bhūt kasēṁ sāṅg adhi-daiv hi tēn tasēṁ
verses.summary: Arjuna asks Krishna: what is Brahman, what is the Self (adhyatma), what is action (karma), what is the realm of beings (adhibhuta), and what is adhidaiva?

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: अधि-यज्ञ कसा कोण ह्या देहीं बोलिला असे । प्रयाणीं हि कसे योगी निग्रही तुज जाणती।
verses.roman: adhi-yajñ kasā kōṇ hyā dēhīm bōlilā asē । prayāṇīṁ hi kasē yōgī nigrahī tuj jāṇatī।
verses.summary: Arjuna asks who and how the adhiyajna is within this body, and how the self-controlled can know Krishna at the moment of death.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ब्रह्म अक्षर तें थोर अध्यात्म निज-भाव तो । भूत-सृष्टी घडे सारी तो जो व्यापार कर्म तें
verses.roman: brahm akṣar tēn thōr adhyātm nija-bhāv tō । bhūta-sr̥ṣṭī ghaḍē sārī tō jō vyāpār karm tēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna replies: Brahman is the supreme imperishable, adhyatma is one's own nature, and karma is the activity that produces all beings.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अधि-भूत विनाशी जें जीवत्व अधि-दैवत । अधि यज्ञ असें मीं चि ह्या देहीं यज्ञ-पूत जो।
verses.roman: adhi-bhūt vināśī jēñ jīvatv adhi-daivat । adhi yajñ asēm mīñ ci hyā dēhīṁ yajña-pūt jō।
verses.summary: Krishna says perishable existence is adhibhuta, the soul-principle is adhidaiva, and he himself is the adhiyajna within this body.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अंत-काळीं हि माझे चि चित्तीं स्मरण राखुनी । देह सोडूनि गेला तो मिळे मज न संशय
verses.roman: anta-kāḷīṁ hi mājhē ci cittīṁ smaraṇ rākhunī । dēh sōḍūni gēlā tō miḷē maj n saṁśay
verses.summary: Krishna says whoever remembers him at the moment of death, leaving the body, attains him—no doubt about it.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: जो-जो आठवुनी भाव शेवटीं देह सोडितो । मिळे त्या त्या चि भावास सदा त्यांत चि रंगला
verses.roman: jō-jō āṭhavunī bhāv śēvaṭīn dēh sōḍitō । miḷē tyā tyā ci bhāvās sadā tyānt ci raṅgalā
verses.summary: Krishna says whatever state one dwells on while leaving the body, that very state one attains, having always been absorbed in it.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: म्हणूनि सगळा काळ मज आठव झुंज तूं मन बुद्धि समर्पूनि मज निःशंक पावसी
verses.roman: mhaṇūni sagaḷā kāḷ maj āṭhav jhuñj tūm man buddhi samarpūni maj niḥśaṅk pāvasī
verses.summary: Krishna tells Arjuna to remember him at all times and fight, offering his mind and intellect, and he will surely reach him.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: आभ्यासीं चित्त जोडूनि योगी अन्य न लक्षुनी । परुषास महा दिव्य पावे संतत चिंतुनी
verses.roman: ābhyāsīñ citt jōḍūni yōgī any n lakṣunī । paruṣās mahā divy pāvē santat cintunī
verses.summary: Krishna says one whose mind is fixed in practice, not straying elsewhere, constantly meditating, reaches the supreme being.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: सर्वज्ञ कर्ता गुरू जो पराण । सूक्ष्माहुनी सूक्ष्म अचिंत्य-रूप ॥ गिळूनि अंधार उजेडला जो । तो चिंतूनियां प्रभु सूर्य-वर्ण
verses.roman: sarvajñ kartā gurū jō parāṇ । sūkṣmāhunī sūkṣm acintya-rūp ॥ giḷūni andhār ujēḍalā jō । tō cintūniyām prabhu sūrya-varṇ
verses.summary: Krishna describes the one to meditate on: all-knowing, ancient, ruler, subtler than the subtle, beyond thought, sunlike, dispelling darkness.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: प्रयाण-काळीं स्थिर चित्त राखे । प्रेमें तसा योग-बळें कसूनि ॥ भू-संग्रामीं प्राण जडूनि ठेवी । तेव्हां मिळे त्या पुरुषास दिव्य ।
verses.roman: prayāṇa-kāḷīṁ sthir citt rākhē । prēmēn tasā yōga-baḷēṅ kasūni ॥ bhū-saṅgrāmīm prāṇ jaḍūni ṭhēvī । tēvhām miḷē tyā puruṣās divy ।
verses.summary: Krishna says one who, at death, holds the mind steady with devotion and yogic power, fixing the breath between the brows, reaches that supreme being.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: जें घोकिती अक्षर वेद-वेत्ते । विरक्त यत्न मिळती जयास ॥ जे ब्रह्मचर्ये पद इच्छिताती । तें सांगतों मी तुज तत्व-सार
verses.roman: jēṅ ghōkitī akṣar vēda-vēttē । virakt yatn miḷatī jayās ॥ jē brahmacaryē pad icchitātī । tēṁ sāṅgatōm mī tuj tatva-sār
verses.summary: Krishna promises to briefly explain the imperishable goal sought by Veda-knowers, the detached, and celibate seekers.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: लावूनि सगळीं द्वारें कोंडुनि मन अंतरीं । मस्तकीं प्राण राखूनि चढला धारणेवरी
verses.roman: lāvūni sagaḷīn dvārēṅ kōṇḍuni man antarīṁ । mastakīm prāṇ rākhūni caḍhalā dhāraṇēvarī
verses.summary: Krishna describes closing all bodily gates, confining the mind within, fixing the breath in the head, established in concentration.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: मुखें ॐ-ब्रह्म उच्चारी अंतरीं मज आठवी । ह्यापरी देह ठेवूनि जाय थोर गतीस तो
verses.roman: mukhēṁ ॐ-brahm uccārī antarīm maj āṭhavī । hyāparī dēh ṭhēvūni jāy thōr gatīs tō
verses.summary: Krishna says one who utters Om while remembering him and then leaves the body attains the highest goal.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अनन्य-चित्त जो नित्य स्मरे मज निरंतर । सदा मिसळला योगी तो सुखें मज पावतो
verses.roman: ananya-citt jō nity smarē maj nirantar । sadā misaḷalā yōgī tō sukhēm maj pāvatō
verses.summary: Krishna says the yogi who constantly remembers him with single-minded focus reaches him easily.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: पावले मोक्ष-सिद्धीस महात्मे मज भेटुनी । दुःखाचें घर तो जन्म न घेती चि अशाश्वत ॥
verses.roman: pāvalē mōkṣa-siddhīs mahātmē maj bhēṭunī । duḥkhācēṅ ghar tō janm n ghētī ci aśāśvat ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says great souls who reach him attain liberation and are not reborn into this impermanent house of sorrow.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ब्रम्हादि लोक ते सारे माघारे घालिती पुन्हां । माझी भेट घडे तेव्हां जन्मणें मग खुंटलें ।
verses.roman: bramhādi lōk tē sārē māghārē ghālitī punhāṁ । mājhī bhēṭ ghaḍē tēvhāñ janmaṇēm mag khuṇṭalēṁ ।
verses.summary: Krishna says all worlds up to Brahma's are subject to return and rebirth, but reaching him ends rebirth.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: होतसे ब्रह्म-देवाचा सहस्र युग तों दिन । तेवढी चि तशी रात्र कालोपासक जाणती
verses.roman: hōtasē brahma-dēvācā sahasr yug tōn din । tēvaḍhī ci taśī rātr kālōpāsak jāṇatī
verses.summary: Krishna says Brahma's day lasts a thousand ages and his night equally long—known by those who understand time.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अव्यक्तापासुनी होती भूतें व्यक्त दिनोदयीं । रात्र होतां लया जाती सगळी मग त्यांत चि
verses.roman: avyaktāpāsunī hōtī bhūtēṁ vyakt dinōdayīṁ । rātr hōtāṁ layā jātī sagaḷī mag tyānt ci
verses.summary: Krishna says at the day's dawn all beings emerge from the unmanifest, and at nightfall they dissolve back into it.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ती चि ती चि पुन्हां भूतें त्यांचे कांहीं न चालतां । दिनांती मरती सारी उदयीं जन्म पावती ।
verses.roman: tī ci tī ci punhām bhūtēn tyāñcē kāṁhīn n cālatāṁ । dināntī maratī sārī udayīñ janm pāvatī ।
verses.summary: Krishna says the same beings repeatedly perish at day's end and are reborn at dawn, helplessly.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अव्यक्त दुसरें तत्व त्या अव्यक्तापलीकडे । नाशतां सगळी भूतें न नाशे जें सनातन
verses.roman: avyakt dusarēn tatv tyā avyaktāpalīkaḍē । nāśatāṁ sagaḷī bhūtēn n nāśē jēṁ sanātan
verses.summary: Krishna says beyond that unmanifest is another eternal unmanifest reality that does not perish when all beings perish.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: त्यास अक्षर हें नाम ती चि शेवटची गती । माझें परम तें धाम जेथूनि परते चि ना
verses.roman: tyās akṣar hēn nām tī ci śēvaṭacī gatī । mājhēm param tēn dhām jēthūni paratē ci nā
verses.summary: Krishna says this imperishable is the supreme goal, his highest abode, from which none return.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: लाभे अनन्य भक्तीनें पार्था परुष थोर तो । ज्यांत ही राहती भूतें ज्यानें विस्तारिलें जग ॥
verses.roman: lābhē anany bhaktīnēm pārthā paruṣ thōr tō । jyānt hī rāhatī bhūtēñ jyānēṁ vistārilēñ jag ॥
verses.summary: Krishna tells Arjuna that supreme being, in whom all beings dwell and who pervades the world, is attained by undivided devotion.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: कोण्या काळीं कसा देह ठेवुनी येथ साधक । संसारीं पडतो किंवां पावतो सिद्धि ऐक तें
verses.roman: kōṇyā kāḷīṅ kasā dēh ṭhēvunī yēth sādhak । saṁsārīm paḍatō kiṁvām pāvatō siddhi aik tēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says he will explain the times at which dying yogis either return to rebirth or attain liberation.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अग्नीनें दिन शुक्लार्ध उत्तरायण जोडुनी । जाय तो गांठतो ब्रह्म शेवटीं ब्रह्म जाणुनी
verses.roman: agnīnēn din śuklārdh uttarāyaṇ jōḍunī । jāy tō gāṇṭhatō brahm śēvaṭīm brahm jāṇunī
verses.summary: Krishna says those who die during fire, day, bright fortnight, and the northern solstice, knowing Brahman, attain Brahman.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: धूमानें रात्र कृष्णार्ध दक्षिणायन जोडुनी । जाय तो परते येथ चंद्र लोकास पावुनी ।
verses.roman: dhūmānēṁ rātr kr̥ṣṇārdh dakṣiṇāyan jōḍunī । jāy tō paratē yēth candr lōkās pāvunī ।
verses.summary: Krishna says those who die during smoke, night, dark fortnight, and the southern solstice reach the moon-world and return.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: उजेड आणि अंधार दोन्ही मार्ग अनादि हे । सुटका करितो एक एक फेऱ्यांत टाकितो
verses.roman: ujēḍ āṇi andhār dōnhī mārg anādi hē । suṭakā karitō ēk ēk phēऱ्yānt ṭākitō
verses.summary: Krishna says light and dark are the two eternal paths—one leads to liberation, the other to return.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: असे हे मार्ग जाणूनि योगी मोह न पावतो । म्हणूनि सर्वदा राहे योगानें जोडिला चि तूं
verses.roman: asē hē mārg jāṇūni yōgī mōh n pāvatō । mhaṇūni sarvadā rāhē yōgānēñ jōḍilā ci tūṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says the yogi who knows these two paths is not deluded; so Arjuna should stay ever united in yoga.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: यज्ञांत दानांत तपांत तैसें जें बोलिलें अध्ययनांत पुण्य । तें लंघितो सर्व चि जाणुनी हें । योगी चढे आद्य पदास थोर
verses.roman: yajñānt dānānt tapānt taisēñ jēm bōlilēṁ adhyayanānt puṇy । tēṁ laṅghitō sarv ci jāṇunī hēṁ । yōgī caḍhē ādy padās thōr
verses.summary: Krishna says the yogi who knows this transcends all merit gained from sacrifices, gifts, austerities, and study, attaining the supreme primal state.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: आतां गुपित हें थोर सांगतों निर्मळा तुज । विज्ञानें कसिलें ज्ञान अशुभांतूनि सोडवी
verses.roman: ātāṅ gupit hēn thōr sāṅgatōn nirmaḷā tuj । vijñānēṅ kasilēñ jñān aśubhāntūni sōḍavī
verses.summary: Krishna tells Arjuna he will reveal the deepest secret—knowledge joined with realization—that frees one from all misfortune.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: राज-विद्या महा-गुह्य उत्तमोत्तम पावन । प्रत्यक्ष हें सुखें लाभे धर्म-सार सनातन
verses.roman: rāja-vidyā mahā-guhy uttamōttam pāvan । pratyakṣ hēṁ sukhēṁ lābhē dharma-sār sanātan
verses.summary: Krishna calls this the king of knowledge, supreme and purifying, directly experienced, easy to practice, and the eternal essence of dharma.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: लोक नास्तिक हा धर्म अश्रद्धेनें न सेविती । मृत्यूची धरिती वाट संसारीं मज सोडुनी ॥
verses.roman: lōk nāstik hā dharm aśraddhēnēn n sēvitī । mr̥tyūcī dharitī vāṭ saṁsārīm maj sōḍunī ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says people lacking faith in this path don't reach him, and instead keep cycling through death and worldly existence.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: मी चि अव्यक्त रुपानें जग हें व्यापिलें असे । माझ्यांत राहती भूतें मी न भूतांत राहतों
verses.roman: mī ci avyakt rupānēñ jag hēṁ vyāpilēṁ asē । mājhyānt rāhatī bhūtēm mī n bhūtānt rāhatōṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says he pervades the whole world in unmanifest form; all beings rest in him, but he does not rest in them.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: न वा भूतें हि माझ्यांत माझा हा दिव्य योग की । करितों धरितों भूते परी त्यांत नसे कुठें
verses.roman: n vā bhūtēṁ hi mājhyānt mājhā hā divy yōg kī । karitōn dharitōm bhūtē parī tyānt nasē kuṭhēṁ
verses.summary: Krishna calls this his mysterious power: beings are not really within him, yet he creates and sustains them while remaining apart.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: आकाशांत महा-वायु सदा सर्वत्र राहतो । माझ्यांत सगळीं भूतें राहती जाण तूं तशीं ।
verses.roman: ākāśānt mahā-vāyu sadā sarvatr rāhatō । mājhyānt sagaḷīm bhūtēṁ rāhatī jāṇ tūn taśīṁ ।
verses.summary: Krishna says that just as the vast wind always moves through space, all beings abide in him.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: कल्पांतीं निजावी भूतें मी माझ्या प्रकृतीमधें । कल्पारंभीं पुन्हां सारीं मी चि जागवितों स्वयें
verses.roman: kalpāntīn nijāvī bhūtēm mī mājhyā prakr̥tīmadhēṁ । kalpārambhīm punhāṁ sārīm mī ci jāgavitōṁ svayēṁ
verses.summary: At the end of a cosmic cycle all beings dissolve into his nature; at the next beginning he sends them forth again.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: हातीं प्रकृति घेऊनि जागवी मी पुन्हां पुन्हां । भूतांचा संघ हा सारा प्रकृतीच्या अधीन जो
verses.roman: hātīm prakr̥ti ghēūni jāgavī mī punhām punhāṁ । bhūtāñcā saṅgh hā sārā prakr̥tīcyā adhīn jō
verses.summary: Krishna says he repeatedly projects this whole multitude of beings, who are helplessly governed by his nature.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: परी हीं सगळीं कर्में बांधूं न शकती मज । उदासीनापरी राहे अनासक्त म्हणूनियां
verses.roman: parī hīṁ sagaḷīṅ karmēm bāndhūn n śakatī maj । udāsīnāparī rāhē anāsakt mhaṇūniyāṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says these actions don't bind him, because he remains detached and indifferent, like an onlooker.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: साक्षी मी प्रकृति-व्दारा उभारीं स्वचराचर । त्यामुळें सर्व सृष्टीची ही घडामोड होतसे ॥
verses.roman: sākṣī mī prakr̥ti-vdārā ubhārīṁ svacarācar । tyāmuḷēṁ sarv sr̥ṣṭīcī hī ghaḍāmōḍ hōtasē ॥
verses.summary: As witness, Krishna oversees nature, which produces all moving and unmoving things; thus the universe revolves.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: मज मानव-रुपांत तुच्छत्वें मूढ देखती । नेणूनि थोरलें रुप जें माझें विश्व-चाळक
verses.roman: maj mānava-rupānt tucchatvēm mūḍh dēkhatī । nēṇūni thōralēṁ rup jēm mājhēṁ viśva-cāḷak
verses.summary: Krishna says the foolish despise him when he takes human form, not knowing his greater nature as ruler of all.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ते आशा-वाद मूढांचे कर्में ज्ञानें हि तीं वृथा । संपत्ति जोडिली ज्यांनीं आसुरी मोह-कारक ।
verses.roman: tē āśā-vād mūḍhāñcē karmēñ jñānēṁ hi tīṁ vr̥thā । sampatti jōḍilī jyānnīṁ āsurī mōha-kārak ।
verses.summary: Their hopes, deeds, and knowledge are vain, says Krishna; deluded, they fall into a confusing demonic disposition.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: दैवी संपत्ति जोडूनि महात्मे भजती मज । अनन्य-भावें जाणूनि मी विश्वारंभ शाश्वत
verses.roman: daivī sampatti jōḍūni mahātmē bhajatī maj । ananya-bhāvēñ jāṇūni mī viśvārambh śāśvat
verses.summary: The great souls of a noble disposition worship him single-mindedly, knowing him as the eternal source of all.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अखंड कीर्तनें माझ्या यत्न-शील दृढ-व्रती । भक्तीनें मज वंदूनि भजतो नित्य जोडिले
verses.roman: akhaṇḍ kīrtanēm mājhyā yatna-śīl dr̥ḍha-vratī । bhaktīnēm maj vandūni bhajatō nity jōḍilē
verses.summary: Constantly praising him, striving with firm vows, bowing in devotion, they worship him always united with him.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: दुसरे ज्ञान-यज्ञानें भजती व्यापका मज ब्रह्म-भावें विवेकानें अविरोधेंचि देखुनी
verses.roman: dusarē jñāna-yajñānēm bhajatī vyāpakā maj brahma-bhāvēṁ vivēkānēṁ avirōdhēñci dēkhunī
verses.summary: Others worship him through the sacrifice of knowledge, seeing him as the all-pervading One in many forms.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: मी चि संकल्प मी चि यज्ञ स्वावलंबन अन्न मी । मंत्र मी हव्य तें मी चि अग्नि मी मी चि अर्पण
verses.roman: mī ci saṅkalp mī ci yajñ svāvalamban ann mī । mantr mī havy tēm mī ci agni mī mī ci arpaṇ
verses.summary: Krishna declares he is the ritual, the offering, the food, the chant, the fire, and the act of offering itself.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: मी ह्या जगास आधार माय बाप वडील मी । मी तिन्ही वेद ॐकार जाणण्या योग्य पावन
verses.roman: mī hyā jagās ādhār māy bāp vaḍīl mī । mī tinhī vēd ॐkār jāṇaṇyā yōgy pāvan
verses.summary: He is the world's support, its father, mother, and grandfather, the three Vedas, the syllable Om, the goal of knowing.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: साक्षी स्वामी सखा भर्ता निवास गति आसरा । करी हरीं धरीं मी चि ठेवा मी बीज अक्षय
verses.roman: sākṣī svāmī sakhā bhartā nivās gati āsarā । karī harīn dharīm mī ci ṭhēvā mī bīj akṣay
verses.summary: Krishna is the witness, lord, friend, sustainer, refuge, origin, dissolution, resting place, and the imperishable seed.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: तापतों सूर्य-रुपें मी सोडितों वृष्टि खेंचितों । मृत्यु मी आणि मी मोक्ष असे आणि नसे हि मी
verses.roman: tāpatōṁ sūrya-rupēm mī sōḍitōṁ vr̥ṣṭi khēñcitōṁ । mr̥tyu mī āṇi mī mōkṣ asē āṇi nasē hi mī
verses.summary: As the sun he gives heat, sends and withholds rain; he is immortality and death, being and non-being alike.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: वेदाभ्यासी सोम-पानें पुनीत । माझ्या यज्ञें इच्छिती स्वर्ग जोडूं ॥ ते पुण्यानें जाउनी इंद्र लोकीं । तेथींचे ते भोगिती दिव्य भोग
verses.roman: vēdābhyāsī sōma-pānēm punīt । mājhyā yajñēṁ icchitī svarg jōḍūṁ ॥ tē puṇyānēñ jāunī indr lōkīṁ । tēthīñcē tē bhōgitī divy bhōg
verses.summary: Those who study the Vedas, drink soma, and seek heaven through sacrifice reach Indra's world and enjoy its pleasures.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: त्या स्वर्गातें भोगुनी ते विशाळ । क्षीणें पुण्यें मृत्यु-लोकास येती ॥ ऐसे निष्ठा ठेवुनी वेद-धर्मीं । येणें-जाणें जोडिती काम-मूढ
verses.roman: tyā svargātēm bhōgunī tē viśāḷ । kṣīṇēm puṇyēm mr̥tyu-lōkās yētī ॥ aisē niṣṭhā ṭhēvunī vēda-dharmīṁ । yēṇēṁ-jāṇēñ jōḍitī kāma-mūḍh
verses.summary: Having enjoyed that vast heaven, when their merit runs out they return to the mortal world; bound to desire, they keep coming and going.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अनन्य-भावें चिंतूनि भजती भक्त जे मज । सदा मिसळले त्यांचा मी याग-क्षेम चालवीं ।
verses.roman: ananya-bhāvēñ cintūni bhajatī bhakt jē maj । sadā misaḷalē tyāñcā mī yāga-kṣēm cālavīṁ ।
verses.summary: Krishna says he personally secures the welfare and provides for those who worship him single-mindedly, ever united with him.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: श्रद्धा-पूर्वक जे कोणी यजिती अन्य दैवतें । यजिती ते हि मातें चि परी मार्गास सोडुनी
verses.roman: śraddhā-pūrvak jē kōṇī yajitī any daivatēṁ । yajitī tē hi mātēñ ci parī mārgās sōḍunī
verses.summary: Even those who worship other deities with faith really worship him, but in a mistaken way.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: भोक्ता मी सर्व यज्ञांचा फल-दाता हि मी चि तो । नेणती तत्व हें माझें म्हणूनि पडती चि ते
verses.roman: bhōktā mī sarv yajñāñcā phala-dātā hi mī ci tō । nēṇatī tatv hēm mājhēm mhaṇūni paḍatī ci tē
verses.summary: He is the enjoyer and giver of fruits of all sacrifices; those who don't recognize his true nature fall back.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: देवांचे भक्त देवांस पितरांचे तयांस चि । भूतांचे भक्त भूतांस माझे हि मज पावती ॥
verses.roman: dēvāñcē bhakt dēvāṁs pitarāñcē tayāṁs ci । bhūtāñcē bhakt bhūtāṁs mājhē hi maj pāvatī ॥
verses.summary: Worshippers of gods go to gods, of ancestors to ancestors, of spirits to spirits; but his devotees come to him.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: पत्र वा पुष्प जो प्रेमें फळ वा जळ दे मज । तें त्या पवित्र भक्ताचें अर्पिलें खाय मी सुखें
verses.roman: patr vā puṣp jō prēmēm phaḷ vā jaḷ dē maj । tēn tyā pavitr bhaktācēṁ arpilēṅ khāy mī sukhēṁ
verses.summary: Whoever offers him a leaf, flower, fruit, or water with love, that pure devotee's offering he gladly accepts.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: जें खासी होमिसी देसी जें जें आचरिसी तप । जें कांहीं करिसी कर्म तें करी मज अर्पण
verses.roman: jēṅ khāsī hōmisī dēsī jēñ jēṁ ācarisī tap । jēṅ kāṁhīṅ karisī karm tēṅ karī maj arpaṇ
verses.summary: Krishna tells Arjuna to offer to him everything he eats, sacrifices, gives, and every austerity and act he does.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अशानें तोडुनी सर्व कर्म-बंध शुभाशुभ । योग-संन्यास सांधूनि मिळसी मज मोकळा
verses.roman: aśānēn tōḍunī sarv karma-bandh śubhāśubh । yōga-sannyās sāndhūni miḷasī maj mōkaḷā
verses.summary: By this you free yourself from the bonds of good and bad action; renouncing through this discipline, you reach him liberated.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: सम मी सर्व भूतांस प्रियाप्रिय नसे मज परी प्रेम-बळें राहे भक्त माझ्यांत त्यांत मी ।
verses.roman: sam mī sarv bhūtāṁs priyāpriy nasē maj parī prēma-baḷēṁ rāhē bhakt mājhyānt tyānt mī ।
verses.summary: Krishna is equal to all beings, favoring none; yet those who worship with love dwell in him and he in them.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: असो मोठा दुराचारी भजे मज अनन्य जो । मानावा तो जसा साधु त्याचा सुंदर निश्चय
verses.roman: asō mōṭhā durācārī bhajē maj anany jō । mānāvā tō jasā sādhu tyācā sundar niścay
verses.summary: Even a great wrongdoer who worships him single-mindedly should be deemed virtuous, for his resolve is right.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: शीघ्र तो होय धर्मात्मा शांति शाश्वत मेळवी । जाण निश्चित तूं माझा भक्त नाश न पावतो ।
verses.roman: śīghr tō hōy dharmātmā śānti śāśvat mēḷavī । jāṇ niścit tūm mājhā bhakt nāś n pāvatō ।
verses.summary: Such a person soon becomes righteous and gains lasting peace; Krishna assures Arjuna his devotee is never destroyed.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: धरुनि आसरा माझा भोळे स्त्री-वैश्य-शूद्र हि । की पाप-योनि जे जीव ते हि मोक्षास पावती
verses.roman: dharuni āsarā mājhā bhōḷē strī-vaiśya-śūdr hi । kī pāpa-yōni jē jīv tē hi mōkṣās pāvatī
verses.summary: Krishna says even the simple, women, vaishyas, shudras, and those of lowly birth reach liberation by taking refuge in him.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: तेथें ब्रह्मर्षि राजर्षि ह्यांची गोष्ट कशास ती । भज तूं मज आल्यास लोकीं दुःख-द नश्वर ।
verses.roman: tēthēm brahmarṣi rājarṣi hyāñcī gōṣṭ kaśās tī । bhaj tūm maj ālyās lōkīn duḥkha-d naśvar ।
verses.summary: How much more, then, the righteous sages and royal seers; Krishna tells Arjuna to worship him in this fleeting, sorrowful world.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: प्रेमानें ध्यास घेऊनि यजी मज नमी मज । असें जोडूनि आत्म्यास मिळसी मज तत्पर ॥
verses.roman: prēmānēn dhyās ghēūni yajī maj namī maj । asēñ jōḍūni ātmyās miḷasī maj tatpar ॥
verses.summary: Fix your mind on him with love, worship and bow to him; thus joining yourself to him you will surely reach him.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: फिरुनि सांगतों ऐक वाक्य उत्तम मी तुज । राखसी श्रवणीं गोडी तुझें हित मी इच्छितों
verses.roman: phiruni sāṅgatōṁ aik vāky uttam mī tuj । rākhasī śravaṇīṅ gōḍī tujhēṁ hit mī icchitōṁ
verses.summary: Krishna tells Arjuna he will speak again, since Arjuna delights in listening; he wishes Arjuna's welfare.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: न देव जाणती माझा प्रभाव न महर्षि हि । सर्वथा मी चि देवांचे महर्षींचें हि मूल कीं
verses.roman: n dēv jāṇatī mājhā prabhāv n maharṣi hi । sarvathā mī ci dēvāñcē maharṣīñcēṁ hi mūl kīṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says neither gods nor great sages know his origin, for he is the source of them all.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: ओळखे जो अजन्मा मी स्वयं-भू-विश्व-चालक । निर्मोह तो मनुष्यांत सुटला पातकांतुनी ।
verses.roman: ōḷakhē jō ajanmā mī svayaṁ-bhū-viśva-cālak । nirmōh tō manuṣyānt suṭalā pātakāntunī ।
verses.summary: Whoever knows Krishna as unborn, beginningless ruler of the world is freed from delusion and all sins.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: बुद्धि निर्मोहता ज्ञान सत्यता शम निग्रह । जन्म नाश सुखें दुःखें लाभालाभ भयाभय
verses.roman: buddhi nirmōhatā jñān satyatā śam nigrah । janm nāś sukhēn duḥkhēṁ lābhālābh bhayābhay
verses.summary: Krishna says qualities like intellect, clarity, knowledge, truth, calm, self-control, birth, death, joy, sorrow, fear and fearlessness arise from him.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: तप दातृत्व संतोष अहिंसा समता क्षमा । माझ्या चि पासुनी भूतीं भाव हे वेगवेगळे ।
verses.roman: tap dātr̥tv santōṣ ahiṁsā samatā kṣamā । mājhyā ci pāsunī bhūtīm bhāv hē vēgavēgaḷē ।
verses.summary: Krishna continues: austerity, charity, contentment, non-violence, equanimity, and forgiveness—these varied states in beings come from him alone.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: महर्षि सात पूर्वींचे चौघे मनु तसे चि ते । माझे संकल्पिले भाव ज्यांची लोकांत ही प्रजा
verses.roman: maharṣi sāt pūrvīñcē caughē manu tasē ci tē । mājhē saṅkalpilē bhāv jyāñcī lōkānt hī prajā
verses.summary: Krishna says the seven ancient sages and the four Manus, from whom these worldly creatures descend, sprang from his mind.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: हा योग-युक्त विस्तार माझा जो नीट ओळखे । त्यास निष्कंप तो योग लाभे ह्यांत न संशय ॥
verses.roman: hā yōga-yukt vistār mājhā jō nīṭ ōḷakhē । tyās niṣkamp tō yōg lābhē hyānt n saṁśay ॥
verses.summary: Whoever truly knows this glory and power of Krishna gains steady union with him—no doubt about it.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: सर्वांचें मूळ माझ्यांत प्रेरणा मजपासुनी । हें ओळखूनि भक्तीनें जाणते भजती मज
verses.roman: sarvāñcēm mūḷ mājhyānt prēraṇā majapāsunī । hēṁ ōḷakhūni bhaktīnēñ jāṇatē bhajatī maj
verses.summary: Krishna says the wise, knowing he is the source and impulse of all, worship him with devotion.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: चित्तें प्राणें जसे मी चि एकमेकांस बोधिती । भरुनि कीर्तनें माझ्या ते आनंदांत खेळती
verses.roman: cittēm prāṇēñ jasē mī ci ēkamēkāṁs bōdhitī । bharuni kīrtanēm mājhyā tē ānandānt khēḷatī
verses.summary: His devotees fix their minds and lives on him, enlighten one another, and delight in singing his praises.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: असे जे रंगले नित्य भजती प्रीति-पूर्वक । त्यांस मी भेटवी मातें देउनी बुद्धि-योग तो
verses.roman: asē jē raṅgalē nity bhajatī prīti-pūrvak । tyāṁs mī bhēṭavī mātēn dēunī buddhi-yōg tō
verses.summary: To those who worship him lovingly and constantly, Krishna gives the discernment by which they reach him.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: करुनि करुणा त्यांची हृदयीं राहुनी स्वयें । तेजस्वी ज्ञान-रुपानें अज्ञान-तम घालवी ॥
verses.roman: karuni karuṇā tyāñcī hr̥dayīṁ rāhunī svayēṁ । tējasvī jñāna-rupānēṁ ajñāna-tam ghālavī ॥
verses.summary: Out of compassion, dwelling in their hearts, he destroys the darkness of ignorance with the light of knowledge.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: पवित्र तूं पर ब्रह्म थोर तें मोक्ष-धाम तूं । आत्मा नित्य अजन्मा तूं विभु देवादि दिव्य तूं
verses.roman: pavitr tūm par brahm thōr tēm mōkṣa-dhām tūṁ । ātmā nity ajanmā tūṁ vibhu dēvādi divy tūṁ
verses.summary: Arjuna addresses Krishna as the pure supreme Brahman, the abode of liberation, the eternal unborn self, all-pervading and beyond the gods.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: ऋषी एक-मुखें गाती तसे अवित देवल । व्यास नारद देवर्षी तूं हि आपण सांगसी
verses.roman: r̥ṣī ēka-mukhēṅ gātī tasē avit dēval । vyās nārad dēvarṣī tūṁ hi āpaṇ sāṅgasī
verses.summary: Arjuna says the sages, and Narada, Asita, Devala, and Vyasa proclaim this, and Krishna himself now confirms it.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: मानितों सत्य हें सारें स्वयें जें सांगसी मज । देव दानव कोणी हि तुझें रुप न जाणती
verses.roman: mānitōṁ saty hēṁ sārēṁ svayēñ jēṁ sāṅgasī maj । dēv dānav kōṇī hi tujhēṁ rup n jāṇatī
verses.summary: Arjuna says he accepts all Krishna tells him as true; neither gods nor demons know Krishna's true form.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: जाणसी हें तुझें तूं चि प्रत्यक्ष पुरुषोत्तमा । देव-देवा जगन्नाथा भूतेशा भूत-भावना ।
verses.roman: jāṇasī hēn tujhēn tūñ ci pratyakṣ puruṣōttamā । dēva-dēvā jagannāthā bhūtēśā bhūta-bhāvanā ।
verses.summary: Arjuna says only Krishna knows himself, addressing him as supreme person, lord of gods and beings, source of all.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: विभूति आपुल्या दिव्य मज निःशेष सांग तूं । ज्यांनीं हें विश्व तूं सारें राहिलास भरुनियां
verses.roman: vibhūti āpulyā divy maj niḥśēṣ sāṅg tūṁ । jyānnīṁ hēṁ viśv tūṁ sārēṁ rāhilās bharuniyāṁ
verses.summary: Arjuna asks Krishna to fully describe his glories by which he pervades the entire universe.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: योगेश्वरा कसें जाणूं चिंतनीं चिंतनीं तुज । कोण्या-कोण्या स्वरुपांत करावें ध्यान मी तुझें
verses.roman: yōgēśvarā kasēñ jāṇūñ cintanīñ cintanīn tuj । kōṇyā-kōṇyā svarupānt karāvēn dhyān mī tujhēṁ
verses.summary: Arjuna asks how he can know Krishna through constant meditation and in which forms he should contemplate him.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: त्या विभूति तसा योग आपुला तो सविस्तर । पुन्हां सांग नव्हे तृप्ति सेवितां वचनामृत ॥
verses.roman: tyā vibhūti tasā yōg āpulā tō savistar । punhāṁ sāṅg navhē tr̥pti sēvitāṁ vacanāmr̥t ॥
verses.summary: Arjuna asks Krishna to recount his glories and power in detail again, for he is never sated hearing his words.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: बरें मी सांगतों दिव्य मुख्य मुख्य चि त्या तुज । माझा विभूति-विस्तार न संपे चि कुठें कधीं
verses.roman: barēm mī sāṅgatōn divy mukhy mukhy ci tyā tuj । mājhā vibhūti-vistār n sampē ci kuṭhēṅ kadhīṁ
verses.summary: Krishna agrees to describe his chief glories, saying the full extent of them has no end.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: राहतों आत्म-रुपानें सर्वांच्या हृदयांत मी । भूत-मात्रास मी मूळ मध्य मी मी चि शेवट ।
verses.roman: rāhatōṁ ātma-rupānēṁ sarvāñcyā hr̥dayānt mī । bhūta-mātrās mī mūḷ madhy mī mī ci śēvaṭ ।
verses.summary: Krishna says he dwells as the self in all hearts and is the beginning, middle, and end of all beings.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: आदित्यांत महा-विष्णु ज्योतिष्मंतांत सूर्य मी । मरीचि मुख्य वायूंत मी नक्षत्रांत चंद्रमा
verses.roman: ādityānt mahā-viṣṇu jyōtiṣmantānt sūry mī । marīci mukhy vāyūnt mī nakṣatrānt candramā
verses.summary: Among Adityas he is Vishnu, among lights the sun, among winds Marichi, among stars the moon.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: मी साम-वेद वेदांत असे देवांत इंद्र मी चेतना मी चि भूतांत मन तें इंद्रियांत मी
verses.roman: mī sāma-vēd vēdānt asē dēvānt indr mī cētanā mī ci bhūtānt man tēṁ indriyānt mī
verses.summary: Among Vedas he is the Sama Veda, among gods Indra, the consciousness in beings, and the mind among the senses.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: कुबेर यक्ष-रक्षांत मी रुद्रांत सदाशिव । वसूंत मी असे अग्नि असे उंचांत मेरु मी
verses.roman: kubēr yakṣa-rakṣānt mī rudrānt sadāśiv । vasūnt mī asē agni asē uñcānt mēru mī
verses.summary: Among Yakshas and Rakshasas he is Kubera, among Rudras Shiva, among Vasus fire, and among peaks Mount Meru.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: पुरोहितांत तूं जाण मुख्य तो मी बृहस्पति । सेनानींत तसा स्कंद जल-राशींत सागर
verses.roman: purōhitānt tūñ jāṇ mukhy tō mī br̥haspati । sēnānīnt tasā skand jala-rāśīnt sāgar
verses.summary: Among priests he is Brihaspati, among generals Skanda, and among bodies of water the ocean.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: मी एकाक्षर वाणींत महर्षींत असे भृगु । जप मी सर्व यज्ञांत मी स्थिरांत हिमालय
verses.roman: mī ēkākṣar vāṇīnt maharṣīnt asē bhr̥gu । jap mī sarv yajñānt mī sthirānt himālay
verses.summary: Among words he is the single syllable Om, among sages Bhrigu, among sacrifices the chant, among immovables the Himalaya.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: सर्व वृक्षांत अश्वत्थ मी देवर्षींत नारद । मी चित्ररथ गंधर्वीं सिद्धीं कपिल मी मुनी
verses.roman: sarv vr̥kṣānt aśvatth mī dēvarṣīnt nārad । mī citrarath gandharvīṁ siddhīṅ kapil mī munī
verses.summary: Among trees the ashvattha, among divine sages Narada, among gandharvas Chitraratha, among the perfected the sage Kapila.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अश्वीं उच्चैःश्रवा जो मी निघालों अमृतांतुनी । ऐरावत गजेंद्रांत मी नरांत नराधिप
verses.roman: aśvīṁ uccaiḥśravā jō mī nighālōṁ amr̥tāntunī । airāvat gajēndrānt mī narānt narādhip
verses.summary: Among horses he is Uchchaihshravas born of nectar, among elephants Airavata, and among men the king.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: मी काम-धेनु गाईंत आयुधीं वज्र मी असे । उत्पत्ति-हेतु मी काम मी सर्पोत्तम वासुकी
verses.roman: mī kāma-dhēnu gāīnt āyudhīṁ vajr mī asē । utpatti-hētu mī kām mī sarpōttam vāsukī
verses.summary: Among cows the wish-cow, among weapons the thunderbolt, the procreative desire, and among serpents Vasuki.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: नागांत शेष मी थोर जळीं वरुण-देवता । पितरीं अर्यमा तो मी ओढणारांत मी यम
verses.roman: nāgānt śēṣ mī thōr jaḷīṁ varuṇa-dēvatā । pitarīṁ aryamā tō mī ōḍhaṇārānt mī yam
verses.summary: Among nagas he is the great Shesha, among water-beings Varuna, among ancestors Aryama, and among controllers Yama.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: असे दैत्यांत प्रल्हाद मोजणारांत काळ मी । श्वापदांत असे सिंह पक्ष्यांत खग-राज मी
verses.roman: asē daityānt pralhād mōjaṇārānt kāḷ mī । śvāpadānt asē siṁh pakṣyānt khaga-rāj mī
verses.summary: Among demons he is Prahlada, among reckoners time, among beasts the lion, and among birds Garuda.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: वेगवंतांत मी वायु शस्त्र-वीरांत राम मी । मत्स्यांत मी असे नक्र नदी गंगा नद्यांत मी ।
verses.roman: vēgavantānt mī vāyu śastra-vīrānt rām mī । matsyānt mī asē nakr nadī gaṅgā nadyānt mī ।
verses.summary: Among the swift he is the wind, among warriors Rama, among fish the crocodile, and among rivers the Ganga.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: सृष्टीचें मी असे मूळ मुख मी ओघ तो हि मी । विद्यांत आत्म-विद्या मी वक्त्यांचा तत्व-वाद मी
verses.roman: sr̥ṣṭīcēm mī asē mūḷ mukh mī ōgh tō hi mī । vidyānt ātma-vidyā mī vaktyāñcā tatva-vād mī
verses.summary: He is the beginning, middle, and end of creation; among sciences the knowledge of the self, and the logic of debaters.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: समासांत असे व्दंव्द अक्षरांत अकार मी । मी चि अक्षय तो काळ विश्व-कर्ता विराट स्वयें
verses.roman: samāsānt asē vdaṁvd akṣarānt akār mī । mī ci akṣay tō kāḷ viśva-kartā virāṭ svayēṁ
verses.summary: Among compounds he is the dvandva, among letters the 'A', the inexhaustible time, and the all-facing creator.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: सर्व-नाशक मी मृत्यू होणारा जन्म मी असे । वाणी श्री कीर्ति नारींत क्षमा मेधा धृति स्मृति
verses.roman: sarva-nāśak mī mr̥tyū hōṇārā janm mī asē । vāṇī śrī kīrti nārīnt kṣamā mēdhā dhr̥ti smr̥ti
verses.summary: He is all-consuming death and the source of future birth; among feminine qualities fame, fortune, speech, memory, intelligence, steadfastness, and forgiveness.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: सामांत मी बृहत्-साम गायत्री मंत्र सार मो । मी मार्गशीर्ष मासांत ऋतूंत फुलाला ऋतू
verses.roman: sāmānt mī br̥hat-sām gāyatrī mantr sār mō । mī mārgaśīrṣ māsānt r̥tūnt phulālā r̥tū
verses.summary: Among Sama hymns the Brihat-sama, among meters the Gayatri, among months Margashirsha, among seasons flowering spring.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: द्यूत मी छळणारांचे तेजस्व्यांतिल तेज मी । सत्व मी सात्विकांतील जय मी आणि निश्चय
verses.roman: dyūt mī chaḷaṇārāñcē tējasvyāntil tēj mī । satv mī sātvikāntīl jay mī āṇi niścay
verses.summary: He is the gambling of cheats, the splendor of the splendid, the victory, resolve, and goodness of the good.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: मी वासुदेव वृष्णींत पांडवांत धनंजय । मुनींत मुनि मी व्यास कवींत उशना कवी
verses.roman: mī vāsudēv vr̥ṣṇīnt pāṇḍavānt dhanañjay । munīnt muni mī vyās kavīnt uśanā kavī
verses.summary: Among the Vrishnis he is Krishna, among Pandavas Arjuna, among sages Vyasa, and among poets Ushana.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: दंड मी दमवंतांचा विजयेच्छूंस धर्म मी । गूढांत मौन मो थोर ज्ञात्यांचे ज्ञान मी असे
verses.roman: daṇḍ mī damavantāñcā vijayēcchūṁs dharm mī । gūḍhānt maun mō thōr jñātyāñcē jñān mī asē
verses.summary: He is the rod of punishers, the statecraft of those seeking victory, silence among secrets, and the knowledge of the wise.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: तसें चि सर्व भूतांचें बीज जें तें हि जाण मी।
verses.roman: tasēñ ci sarv bhūtāñcēm bīj jēn tēṁ hi jāṇ mī।
verses.summary: Krishna says he is also the seed of all beings.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: माझ्याविण नसे कांहीं लेश-मात्र चराचरीं । माझ्या दिव्य विभूतींस नसे अंत कुठें चि तो
verses.roman: mājhyāviṇ nasē kāṁhīṁ lēśa-mātr carācarīṁ । mājhyā divy vibhūtīṁs nasē ant kuṭhēñ ci tō
verses.summary: Krishna says nothing moving or unmoving exists without him; there is no end to his glories.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: विभूति-युक्त जी वस्तू लक्ष्मीवंत उदात्त वा । माझ्या चि किरणांतूनि निघाली जाण ती असे।
verses.roman: vibhūti-yukt jī vastū lakṣmīvant udātt vā । mājhyā ci kiraṇāntūni nighālī jāṇ tī asē।
verses.summary: Whatever is glorious, prosperous, or noble, Krishna says, springs from a fragment of his splendor.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: अथवा काय हें फार जाणूनि करिशील तूं । एकांशें विश्व हें सारें व्यापूनि उरलों चि मी ॥
verses.roman: athavā kāy hēm phār jāṇūni kariśīl tūṁ । ēkāṁśēṁ viśv hēṁ sārēṁ vyāpūni uralōñ ci mī ॥
verses.summary: Krishna concludes there's no need to know all this in detail: he pervades and supports the whole universe with a single fraction of himself.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: करुनि करुणा माझी बोलिलास रहस्य जें । त्या थोर आत्म-विद्येनें माझा हा मोह फेडिला
verses.roman: karuni karuṇā mājhī bōlilās rahasy jēṁ । tyā thōr ātma-vidyēnēm mājhā hā mōh phēḍilā
verses.summary: Arjuna says Krishna's compassionate teaching about the self has cleared away his confusion.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: उत्पत्ति-नाश भूतांचे ऐकिले मी सविस्तर । कळला तुजपासूनि अभंग महिमा तुझा ।
verses.roman: utpatti-nāś bhūtāñcē aikilē mī savistar । kaḷalā tujapāsūni abhaṅg mahimā tujhā ।
verses.summary: Arjuna says he has heard in detail about the origin and dissolution of beings, and learned of Krishna's imperishable greatness.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: तुझें तें ईश्वरी रुप मानितों सांगसी जसें । तें चि मी इच्छितों पाहूं प्रत्यक्ष परुषोत्तमा
verses.roman: tujhēn tēṁ īśvarī rup mānitōṁ sāṅgasī jasēṁ । tēñ ci mī icchitōm pāhūm pratyakṣ paruṣōttamā
verses.summary: Arjuna says he believes Krishna's description of his cosmic form and now wishes to see it directly.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: तूं जरी मानिसी शक्य मज तें रुप पाहणें । तरी योगेश्रा देवा दाखवी ते चि शाश्वत ॥
verses.roman: tūñ jarī mānisī śaky maj tēṁ rup pāhaṇēṁ । tarī yōgēśrā dēvā dākhavī tē ci śāśvat ॥
verses.summary: Arjuna asks Krishna, if he thinks it possible, to show him that eternal universal form.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: पहा दिव्य तशी माझीं रुपें शत-सहस्र तूं । नाना प्रकार आकार वर्ण ज्यांत विचित्र चि
verses.roman: pahā divy taśī mājhīṁ rupēṁ śata-sahasr tūṁ । nānā prakār ākār varṇ jyānt vicitr ci
verses.summary: Krishna tells Arjuna to behold his hundreds and thousands of forms, of varied kinds, shapes, and colors.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: असु वायु पहा रुद्र तसे आदित्य अश्विनी । पहा अनेक आश्चर्यें कधीं कोणी न पाहिलीं
verses.roman: asu vāyu pahā rudr tasē ādity aśvinī । pahā anēk āścaryēṅ kadhīṅ kōṇī n pāhilīṁ
verses.summary: Krishna says to see the Adityas, Vasus, Rudras, Ashvins, and Maruts—many marvels never seen before.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: इथें आज पहा सारें विश्व तूं सचराचर । माझ्या देहांत एकत्र इच्छा-दर्शन हें तुज
verses.roman: ithēṁ āj pahā sārēṁ viśv tūṁ sacarācar । mājhyā dēhānt ēkatr icchā-darśan hēn tuj
verses.summary: Krishna invites Arjuna to see the whole moving and unmoving universe gathered in his body, along with anything else he wishes.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: परी तूं चर्म-चक्षूनें पाहूं न शकसी मज । घे दिव्य दृष्टि ही माझी ईश्वरी योग तूं पहा
verses.roman: parī tūñ carma-cakṣūnēm pāhūn n śakasī maj । ghē divy dr̥ṣṭi hī mājhī īśvarī yōg tūm pahā
verses.summary: Krishna says Arjuna cannot see him with ordinary eyes, so he grants him divine sight to behold his power.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: महा-योगेश्वरें कृष्णें राया बोलूनि ह्यापरी । दाविलें तेथ पार्थास थोरलें रूप ईश्वरी ।
verses.roman: mahā-yōgēśvarēṅ kr̥ṣṇēṁ rāyā bōlūni hyāparī । dāvilēn tēth pārthās thōralēṁ rūp īśvarī ।
verses.summary: Sanjaya tells Dhritarashtra that, having spoken thus, Krishna showed Arjuna his great cosmic form.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: बहु-डोळे मुखें ज्यांत दर्शनें बहु अदभुत । बहु दिव्य अलंकार सज्ज दिव्यायुधें बहु
verses.roman: bahu-ḍōḷē mukhēñ jyānt darśanēm bahu adabhut । bahu divy alaṅkār sajj divyāyudhēm bahu
verses.summary: Sanjaya describes the form with many eyes and mouths, wondrous sights, many ornaments, and many raised weapons.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: दिव्य वस्त्रें फुलें गंध लेउनी सर्वतोपरी । आश्चर्यें भरला देव विश्व-व्यापी अनंत तो
verses.roman: divy vastrēm phulēṅ gandh lēunī sarvatōparī । āścaryēm bharalā dēv viśva-vyāpī anant tō
verses.summary: Sanjaya describes the all-pervading, infinite form wearing divine garments, garlands, and fragrances, full of wonders.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: प्रभा सहस्र-सूर्यांची नभीं एकवटे जरी । तरी त्या थोर देवाच्या प्रभेशीं न जुळे चि ती
verses.roman: prabhā sahasra-sūryāñcī nabhīṁ ēkavaṭē jarī । tarī tyā thōr dēvācyā prabhēśīn n juḷē ci tī
verses.summary: Sanjaya says even the light of a thousand suns combined would not match the radiance of that great form.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: सारे जगांतले भेद तेव्हां काळवले जसे । देहांत देव-देवाच्या देखिले तेथ अर्जुनें ।
verses.roman: sārē jagāntalē bhēd tēvhāṅ kāḷavalē jasē । dēhānt dēva-dēvācyā dēkhilē tēth arjunēṁ ।
verses.summary: Sanjaya says Arjuna saw the whole manifold universe gathered together in the body of that God.

verses.speaker: संजय
verses.text: मग विस्मित तो झाला अंगीं रोमांच दाटले । प्रभूस हात जोडूनि बोलिला नत-मस्तक
verses.roman: mag vismit tō jhālā aṅgīṁ rōmāñc dāṭalē । prabhūs hāt jōḍūni bōlilā nata-mastak
verses.summary: Sanjaya says Arjuna, amazed and with hair standing on end, bowed his head and spoke with folded hands.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: देखें प्रभो देव तुझ्या शरीरीं । कोंदाटले सर्व चि भूत-संघ । पदमासनीं ध्यान धरी विधाता । ऋषींमधें खेळत दिव्य सर्प
verses.roman: dēkhēm prabhō dēv tujhyā śarīrīṁ । kōndāṭalē sarv ci bhūta-saṅgh । padamāsanīn dhyān dharī vidhātā । r̥ṣīmmadhēṅ khēḷat divy sarp
verses.summary: Arjuna says he sees all beings within Krishna's body—Brahma seated on his lotus, sages, and divine serpents.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: लेऊनि डोळे सुख हात पोट । जिथें जिथें तूं चि अनंत-मूर्तें । विश्वेश्वरा शेवट मध्य मूळ । तुझ्या न मी देखत विश्व-रुपी
verses.roman: lēūni ḍōḷē sukh hāt pōṭ । jithēñ jithēn tūñ ci ananta-mūrtēṁ । viśvēśvarā śēvaṭ madhy mūḷ । tujhyā n mī dēkhat viśva-rupī
verses.summary: Arjuna sees infinite arms, bellies, eyes, and faces everywhere, but no beginning, middle, or end of the universal form.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: प्रभो गदा-चक्र-किरीट-धारी । प्रकाश सर्वत्र तुझा प्रचंड । डोळे न पाहूं शकती अपार । ज्यांतूनि हे पेटत अग्नि-सूर्य ।
verses.roman: prabhō gadā-cakra-kirīṭa-dhārī । prakāś sarvatr tujhā pracaṇḍ । ḍōḷē n pāhūṁ śakatī apār । jyāntūni hē pēṭat agni-sūry ।
verses.summary: Arjuna sees Krishna bearing mace, discus, and crown, blazing immeasurably so the eyes can scarcely look, like fire and sun.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: तूं थोर तें अक्षर जाणण्याचें । तुझा चि आधार जगास अंतीं । तूं राखिसी शाश्वत-धर्म नित्य । मी मानितों तूं परमात्म-तत्व ।
verses.roman: tūn thōr tēṁ akṣar jāṇaṇyācēṁ । tujhā ci ādhār jagās antīṁ । tūṁ rākhisī śāśvata-dharm nity । mī mānitōn tūm paramātma-tatv ।
verses.summary: Arjuna says Krishna is the supreme imperishable, the world's foundation, guardian of eternal dharma, the supreme Self.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: किती भुजा वीर्य किती पसारा । डोळे कसे उज्वल चंद्र-सूर्य हा पेटला अग्नि तुझ्या मुखांत । तूं ताविसी सर्व चि आत्म-तेजें
verses.roman: kitī bhujā vīry kitī pasārā । ḍōḷē kasē ujval candra-sūry hā pēṭalā agni tujhyā mukhānt । tūn tāvisī sarv ci ātma-tējēṁ
verses.summary: Arjuna describes endless arms, eyes like sun and moon, fire blazing from his mouth, heating the whole universe.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: दाही दिशा विस्तृत अंतराळ । व्यापूनि तूं एक चि राहिलासी ॥ पाहूनि हें अदभुत उग्र रूप । तिन्ही जगें व्याकुळली उदारा
verses.roman: dāhī diśā vistr̥t antarāḷ । vyāpūni tūṁ ēk ci rāhilāsī ॥ pāhūni hēṁ adabhut ugr rūp । tinhī jagēṁ vyākuḷalī udārā
verses.summary: Arjuna says Krishna alone fills all directions and space; seeing this fierce, wondrous form, the three worlds tremble.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: हे देव सारे रिघती तुझ्यांत । कोणी भयें प्रार्थित बद्ध-हस्त ॥ मांगल्य-गीतें तुज सिद्ध संत । परोपरी आळविती समस्त
verses.roman: hē dēv sārē righatī tujhyānt । kōṇī bhayēm prārthit baddha-hast ॥ māṅgalya-gītēn tuj siddh sant । parōparī āḷavitī samast
verses.summary: Arjuna says hosts of gods enter Krishna, some praying in fear with folded hands, while sages and saints sing praises.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: आदित्य विश्वे वसु रुद्र साध्य । कुमार दोघे पितृ-देव वायु ॥ गंधर्व दैत्यांसह यक्ष सिद्ध । सारे कसे विस्मित पाहताती ।
verses.roman: ādity viśvē vasu rudr sādhy । kumār dōghē pitr̥-dēv vāyu ॥ gandharv daityāṁsah yakṣ siddh । sārē kasē vismit pāhatātī ।
verses.summary: Arjuna lists Adityas, Vasus, Rudras, Sadhyas, Maruts, ancestors, and various celestial beings all gazing in amazement.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: अफाट हें रूप असंख्य डोळे । मुखें भुजा ऊरु असंख्य पाय ॥ असंख्य पोटें विकराळ दाढा । ह्या दर्शनें व्याकुल लोक मी हि
verses.roman: aphāṭ hēṁ rūp asaṅkhy ḍōḷē । mukhēm bhujā ūru asaṅkhy pāy ॥ asaṅkhy pōṭēṁ vikarāḷ dāḍhā । hyā darśanēṁ vyākul lōk mī hi
verses.summary: Arjuna says seeing the vast form with countless eyes, mouths, arms, legs, bellies, and terrible teeth, the worlds and he are shaken.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: भेदूनि आकाश भरूनि रंगीं । फाडूनि डोळे उघडूनि तोंडें । तूं पेटलासी बघ जीव माझा । भ्याला न देखे शम आणि धीर
verses.roman: bhēdūni ākāś bharūni raṅgīṁ । phāḍūni ḍōḷē ughaḍūni tōṇḍēṁ । tūm pēṭalāsī bagh jīv mājhā । bhyālā n dēkhē śam āṇi dhīr
verses.summary: Arjuna says the blazing form, touching the sky and many-colored, with gaping mouths and eyes, terrifies him and robs his peace.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: कराळ दाढा विकराळ तोंडें । कल्पांत अग्नी-सम देखतां चि । दिग्मूढ झालों सुख तें पळालें । प्रसन्न हो की जग हें तुझें चि
verses.roman: karāḷ dāḍhā vikarāḷ tōṇḍēṁ । kalpānt agnī-sam dēkhatāñ ci । digmūḍh jhālōṁ sukh tēm paḷālēṁ । prasann hō kī jag hēn tujhēñ ci
verses.summary: Seeing the dreadful teeth and mouths like the fire of dissolution, Arjuna is bewildered and begs Krishna's grace.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: अहा कसे हे धृतराष्ट्र-पुत्र । घेऊनियां राज समूह सारे । हे भीष्म हे द्रोण तसा चि कर्ण । हे आमुचे वीर हि मुख्य मुख्य
verses.roman: ahā kasē hē dhr̥tarāṣṭra-putr । ghēūniyāṁ rāj samūh sārē । hē bhīṣm hē drōṇ tasā ci karṇ । hē āmucē vīr hi mukhy mukhy
verses.summary: Arjuna sees Dhritarashtra's sons with their allied kings, along with Bhishma, Drona, Karna, and chief warriors of his own side.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: जाती त्वरेनें चि तुझ्या मुखांत । भयाण जी भ्यासुर ज्यांत दाढा । दातांत कांही शिरलीं शिरें जी । त्यांचें जसें पीठ चि पाहतों मी ।
verses.roman: jātī tvarēnēñ ci tujhyā mukhānt । bhayāṇ jī bhyāsur jyānt dāḍhā । dātānt kāṁhī śiralīṁ śirēñ jī । tyāñcēñ jasēm pīṭh ci pāhatōm mī ।
verses.summary: Arjuna sees them rushing into Krishna's terrible toothed mouths, some with heads crushed to powder between the teeth.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: जसे नद्यांचे सगळे प्रवाह । वेगें समुद्रांत चि धांव घेती । तसे तुझ्या हे जळत्या मुखांत धांवूनि जाती नर-वीर सारे
verses.roman: jasē nadyāñcē sagaḷē pravāh । vēgēṁ samudrānt ci dhāṁv ghētī । tasē tujhyā hē jaḷatyā mukhānt dhāṁvūni jātī nara-vīr sārē
verses.summary: Arjuna says that like rivers rushing into the sea, these warriors rush into Krishna's flaming mouths.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: भरुनियां वेग जसे पतंग । घेती उडया अग्नि-मुखीं मराया । तसे चि हे लोक तुझ्या मुखांत । घेती उडया वेग-भरें मराया ।
verses.roman: bharuniyāṁ vēg jasē pataṅg । ghētī uḍayā agni-mukhīm marāyā । tasē ci hē lōk tujhyā mukhānt । ghētī uḍayā vēga-bharēm marāyā ।
verses.summary: Arjuna says that like moths plunging into a flame to die, these people rush headlong into Krishna's mouths to perish.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: समस्त लोकांस गिळूनि ओंठ । तूं चाटितोसी जळत्या जिभांनीं । वेढूनि विश्वास समग्र तेजें । भाजे प्रभो उग्र तुझी प्रभा ही ।
verses.roman: samast lōkāṁs giḷūni ōṇṭh । tūñ cāṭitōsī jaḷatyā jibhānnīṁ । vēḍhūni viśvās samagr tējēṁ । bhājē prabhō ugr tujhī prabhā hī ।
verses.summary: Arjuna says Krishna devours all the worlds with flaming tongues, his fierce radiance scorching the entire universe.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन
verses.text: सांगा असां कोण तुम्हीं भयाण । नमूं तुम्हां देव-वरा न कोपा । जाणावया उत्सुक आदि देवा । ध्यानीं न ये की करणी कशी ही ॥
verses.roman: sāṅgā asāṅ kōṇ tumhīm bhayāṇ । namūn tumhān dēva-varā n kōpā । jāṇāvayā utsuk ādi dēvā । dhyānīn n yē kī karaṇī kaśī hī ॥
verses.summary: Arjuna asks who this terrible form is, bows to it, and seeks to understand it, unable to grasp this activity.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: मी काळ लोकांतक वाढलेला । भक्षावया सिद्ध इथें जनांस । हे नष्ट होतील तुझ्या विना हि । झाले उभे हे उभयत्र वीर
verses.roman: mī kāḷ lōkāntak vāḍhalēlā । bhakṣāvayā siddh ithēñ janāṁs । hē naṣṭ hōtīl tujhyā vinā hi । jhālē ubhē hē ubhayatr vīr
verses.summary: Krishna says he is Time, grown mighty to destroy these people; even without Arjuna these warriors will perish.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: म्हणूनि तूं ऊठ मिळीव कीर्ति । जिंकूनि निष्कंटक राज्य भोगी । मी मारिले हे सगळे चि आधीं । निमित्त हो केवळ सव्य-साची
verses.roman: mhaṇūni tūṁ ūṭh miḷīv kīrti । jiṅkūni niṣkaṇṭak rājy bhōgī । mī mārilē hē sagaḷē ci ādhīṁ । nimitt hō kēvaḷ savya-sācī
verses.summary: Krishna tells Arjuna to rise, win glory, and enjoy the kingdom; the warriors are already slain by him—Arjuna is just the instrument.

verses.speaker: श्री भगवान्
verses.text: द्रोणास भीष्मास जयद्रथास । कर्णादि वीरांस रणांगणांत । मी मारिलेल्यांस फिरुनि मारी । निःशंक झुंजे जय तो तुझा चि ॥
verses.roman: drōṇās bhīṣmās jayadrathās । karṇādi vīrāṁs raṇāṅgaṇānt । mī mārilēlyāṁs phiruni mārī । niḥśaṅk jhuñjē jay tō tujhā ci ॥
verses.summary: Krishna says he has already killed Drona, Bhishma, Jayadratha, Karna and others; Arjuna should fight without fear and win.

verses.speaker: संजय
